info.title: API Reference
info.description: API reference for the StackRox Security Platform

get.summary: ListAdministrationEvents returns a paginated list of administration events matching the given filter.
get.description:
Results are sorted by last update time descending (most recent first) by default.
The maximum page size is 1000 events.
Filters support time range (from/until based on creation time), domain, resource type,
event type, and level. All specified filters are ANDed.
Requires read access to the Administration resource.

parameters.name: filter.from
parameters.description: Matches events with last_occurred_at after a specific timestamp, i.e. the lower boundary.

parameters.name: filter.until
parameters.description: Matches events with last_occurred_at before a specific timestamp, i.e. the upper boundary.

parameters.name: filter.domain
parameters.description: Matches events from a specific domain.

parameters.name: filter.resourceType
parameters.description: Matches events associated with a specific resource type.

parameters.name: filter.type
parameters.description: Matches events based on their type.

parameters.name: filter.level
parameters.description: Matches events based on their level.

get.summary: GetAdministrationEvent returns the administration event with the given ID.
get.description: Requires read access to the Administration resource.

get.summary: CountAdministrationEvents returns the number of events matching the given filter.
get.description:
The filter supports time range (from/until), domain, resource type, event type, and level.
All specified filters are ANDed. Requires read access to the Administration resource.

parameters.name: filter.from
parameters.description: Matches events with last_occurred_at after a specific timestamp, i.e. the lower boundary.

parameters.name: filter.until
parameters.description: Matches events with last_occurred_at before a specific timestamp, i.e. the upper boundary.

parameters.name: filter.domain
parameters.description: Matches events from a specific domain.

parameters.name: filter.resourceType
parameters.description: Matches events associated with a specific resource type.

parameters.name: filter.type
parameters.description: Matches events based on their type.

parameters.name: filter.level
parameters.description: Matches events based on their level.

get.summary:
GetCurrentSecuredUnitsUsage returns the current secured units usage
metrics values.
get.description:
The secured units metrics are collected from all connected clusters every
5 minutes, so the returned result includes data for the connected
clusters accurate to about these 5 minutes, and potentially some outdated
data for the disconnected clusters.
Requires read access to the Administration resource.

get.summary:
GetMaxSecuredUnitsUsage returns the maximum, i.e. peak, secured units
usage observed during a given time range, together with the time when
this maximum was aggregated and stored.
get.description:
The usage metrics are continuously collected from all the connected
clusters. The maximum values are kept for some period of time in memory,
and then, periodically, are stored to the database.
The last data from disconnected clusters are taken into account.
The from field defaults to the Unix epoch and to defaults to the current time.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if from is not before to.
Requires read access to the Administration resource.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: from is the start of the time range (inclusive). If not set, defaults to the Unix epoch.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
to is the end of the time range (inclusive). If not set, defaults to the current time.
Must be after from; returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if from >= to.

get.summary: ListAlerts returns a paginated list of slim alert summaries matching the query.
get.description:
Results are sorted by violation time (descending) by default. When no pagination
is specified, up to 1000 alerts are returned. Use the pagination field to
retrieve additional pages.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters alerts using StackRox search syntax.
Supports fields such as Severity, Cluster, Namespace, Policy, Violation State,
Lifecycle Stage, and Category.
Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"
If empty, matches all alerts.

delete.summary: DeleteAlerts permanently removes resolved alerts matching a search query.
delete.description:
The query must include "Violation State:RESOLVED"; attempting to delete
non-resolved alerts returns INVALID_ARGUMENT. A scoping query is required;
omitting query returns an error.

Set confirm=false (or omit it) to perform a dry run that reports how many
alerts would be deleted without removing any. Set confirm=true to execute
the deletion.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query is missing, unparseable, or targets
non-resolved alerts.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description:
confirm must be set to true to perform the deletion.
When false (or omitted), the response reflects a dry run showing how many
alerts would be deleted without actually removing them.

patch.summary: ResolveAlerts resolves all runtime alerts matching a search query in bulk.
patch.description:
Only alerts in the RUNTIME lifecycle stage are resolved; alerts from other
lifecycle stages that match the query are silently skipped.
Alerts are resolved in batches of 100 and notifiers are triggered for each.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

get.summary: GetAlertsCounts returns alert counts broken down by severity, optionally grouped by category or cluster.
get.description:
Use group_by=CATEGORY to segment counts by policy category, or group_by=CLUSTER to segment
by cluster name. group_by=UNSET (default) returns a single aggregated bucket.
Counts reflect all alerts matching the embedded query filter.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed or group_by is unrecognized.

parameters.name: request.query
parameters.description:
query filters alerts using StackRox search syntax.
Supports fields such as Severity, Cluster, Namespace, Policy, Violation State,
Lifecycle Stage, and Category.
Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"
If empty, matches all alerts.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description:
group_by controls how alert counts are aggregated in the response.

 - UNSET: UNSET returns all alert counts in a single ungrouped bucket.
 - CATEGORY: CATEGORY groups alert counts by policy category (e.g. "Vulnerability Management").
 - CLUSTER: CLUSTER groups alert counts by cluster name.

get.summary: GetAlertsGroup returns active alert counts grouped by policy.
get.description:
Each entry in the response lists a policy alongside the number of active
alerts currently triggered by that policy. Results are sorted by policy name.
Useful for building a policy-centric view of the alert landscape.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters alerts using StackRox search syntax.
Supports fields such as Severity, Cluster, Namespace, Policy, Violation State,
Lifecycle Stage, and Category.
Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"
If empty, matches all alerts.

get.summary: GetAlertTimeseries returns alert creation and resolution events as a timeseries, grouped by cluster and severity.
get.description:
Each event records a Unix millisecond timestamp and whether the alert was
CREATED (became active) or REMOVED (resolved) at that moment. Events within
each severity bucket are sorted chronologically. Useful for rendering alert
trend charts over time.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters alerts using StackRox search syntax.
Supports fields such as Severity, Cluster, Namespace, Policy, Violation State,
Lifecycle Stage, and Category.
Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"
If empty, matches all alerts.

get.summary: GetAlert returns the full alert detail for a single alert by ID.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if no alert with the given ID exists.

patch.summary: ResolveAlert marks a single alert as RESOLVED.
patch.description:
Optionally, setting add_to_baseline=true adds the processes that triggered
the alert to the deployment's process baseline and syncs the updated baseline
to the cluster, suppressing future alerts for those processes.

Only runtime and attempted alerts can be resolved; deploy-time alerts transition
to resolved state automatically when the violation is remediated.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no alert with the given ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the alert to resolve.

get.summary: CountAlerts returns the total number of alerts matching a search query.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production").
An empty query counts all alerts. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query
cannot be parsed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: GetAPITokens returns metadata for all API tokens, optionally filtered by revocation status.
get.description:
Use the revoked field to filter for active or revoked tokens only.
Requires read access to the Integration resource.

parameters.name: revoked
parameters.description:
revoked filters tokens by their revocation status.
Set to true to return only revoked tokens; false to return only active tokens.
Omit to return all tokens.

post.summary: GenerateToken creates a new API token assigned to one or more roles.
post.description:
The token name must be non-empty. All requested roles must exist and must not exceed
the caller's own permissions (privilege escalation prevention).
The raw token value is returned only in this response; it cannot be retrieved later.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the name is empty, both role and roles are set, or a role does not exist.
Returns NOT_AUTHORIZED if any requested role has more permissions than the caller.
Requires read access to the Access resource and write access to the Integration resource.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: GenerateTokenRequest specifies the parameters for creating a new API token.

get.summary: ListAllowedTokenRoles returns the role names the caller is permitted to assign to a new API token.
get.description:
Roles with more permissions than the caller holds are excluded (privilege escalation prevention).
The built-in "None" role is excluded. Results are sorted alphabetically.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

patch.summary: RevokeToken marks the API token with the given ID as revoked, immediately invalidating it.
patch.description:
Revocation is permanent; revoked tokens cannot be reinstated.
Returns an error if the ID is empty or no token with that ID exists.
Requires write access to the Integration resource.

get.summary: GetAPIToken returns the metadata for the API token with the given ID.
get.description:
Note: the raw token value is never returned; only metadata is available after creation.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty or no token with that ID exists.
Requires read access to the Integration resource.

get.summary: ListAuthMachineToMachineConfigs returns all configured machine-to-machine token exchange rules.
get.description: Requires read access to the Access resource.

post.summary: AddAuthMachineToMachineConfig creates a new machine-to-machine token exchange config.
post.description:
The config.id field must be empty; a UUID is assigned by the server.
Validates token_expiration_duration (1 minute to 24 hours), issuer URL (must use HTTPS),
and that all mapping value_expression fields are valid RE2 regular expressions.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

post.summary:
ExchangeAuthMachineToMachineToken exchanges a third-party OIDC identity token for a
Central access token, based on configured machine-to-machine rules.
post.description:
The issuer of the provided id_token is extracted and matched against configured M2M configs.
Token claim values are then evaluated against the configured mappings to resolve a role.
At least one mapping must match for a Central access token to be issued.

Returns NO_CREDENTIALS if no M2M config is registered for the token's issuer.
This endpoint allows anonymous calls (no prior authentication required).

put.summary:
UpdateAuthMachineToMachineConfig updates an existing machine-to-machine token exchange config.
In case the auth machine to machine config does not exist, a new one will be created.
put.description:
The config.id field must be set to the ID of the config to update.
Applies the same validation as AddAuthMachineToMachineConfig.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: config.id
parameters.description:
UUID of the config.
Note that when adding a machine to machine config, this field should not be set.

get.summary: GetAuthMachineToMachineConfig returns the machine-to-machine config with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no config with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

delete.summary:
DeleteAuthMachineToMachineConfig removes the machine-to-machine config with the given ID.
In case a specified auth machine to machine config does not exist is deleted, no error will be returned.
delete.description: Requires write access to the Access resource.

get.summary: GetAuthStatus returns the authentication status of the caller making the request.
get.description:
The response includes the caller's identity (user ID or service identity), token expiry,
associated auth provider (with sensitive config fields stripped), resolved roles and
permissions, and raw identity-provider attributes.

This endpoint allows anonymous calls; unauthenticated requests receive HTTP 401.

get.summary: GetAuthProviders returns all auth providers, optionally filtered by name and/or type.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: name filters results to providers whose name exactly matches this value.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: type filters results to providers of this type (e.g. "oidc", "saml2", "userpki", "iap", "openshift").

post.summary: PostAuthProvider creates a new auth provider.
post.description:
The provider.id and provider.login_url fields must be empty; they are assigned by the server.
The provider.name must be non-empty. The built-in "basic" provider type cannot be created.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description:
provider holds the new auth provider to create. The id and login_url fields must be empty;
they are assigned by the server.

post.summary: ExchangeToken exchanges an external identity provider token for a Central access token.
post.description:
Used as part of the browser login flow after an OAuth/OIDC callback. The external_token,
type, and state from the IdP callback are submitted here. On success a Central JWT is returned.
When state contains an internal test-mode marker, the response returns the resolved user auth
status instead of a token, allowing preview of role assignments without issuing a credential.
This endpoint is publicly accessible (no authentication required).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: ExchangeTokenRequest carries the external identity provider token to be exchanged for a Central token.

get.summary: GetAuthProvider returns the auth provider with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no provider with the specified ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the auth provider to retrieve.

delete.summary: DeleteAuthProvider removes an auth provider and all its associated group mappings.
delete.description:
The built-in "basic" provider cannot be deleted.
When force is false, deletion is blocked if the provider has associated group mappings.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no provider with the specified ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

put.summary: PutAuthProvider fully replaces an existing auth provider.
put.description:
The request body must include the full provider definition including id.
Config fields omitted from the request are merged from the existing provider.
The built-in "basic" provider cannot be modified.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no provider with the specified ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty or validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

patch.summary: UpdateAuthProvider partially updates an existing auth provider's name or enabled status.
patch.description:
Only the name and enabled fields can be modified via this endpoint. For full replacement,
use PutAuthProvider. The built-in "basic" provider cannot be modified.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no provider with the specified ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty or validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the auth provider to update.

get.summary: ListAvailableProviderTypes returns the auth provider types that can be created on this installation.
get.description:
The list is derived from registered backend factories. The built-in "basic" provider type
is excluded. Results are sorted alphabetically by type name.
This endpoint is publicly accessible (no authentication required).

get.summary: GetLoginAuthProviders returns the minimal auth provider information needed to render the login screen.
get.description:
Only returns enabled, visible providers that have a registered backend factory.
Results are sorted by name; the built-in basic provider appears last.
This endpoint is publicly accessible (no authentication required).

get.summary: GetExternalBackups returns all external backup configurations.
get.description:
Sensitive credential fields are replaced with a placeholder in each
returned configuration. The response is not paginated.

post.summary: PostExternalBackup creates a new external backup configuration.
post.description:
The id field must be empty; a new ID is assigned. The configuration is
validated (name, schedule, backups_to_keep) and registered with the
integration health reporter before being persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is set, if required fields are
missing or invalid, or if the schedule expression is malformed.

post.summary:
TestExternalBackup verifies that the given backup configuration is
reachable and correctly set up, treating all credential fields as
authoritative (no stored credential reconciliation).
post.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation or the connectivity test fails.

post.summary:
TestUpdatedExternalBackup verifies that the given external backup
configuration is reachable with optional stored credential reconciliation.
post.description:
When update_password is false, credential fields in the request are
replaced with the stored credentials before the test is performed.
When update_password is true, the provided credentials are used directly.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation or the connectivity test fails.
Returns NOT_FOUND if update_password is false and the configuration does
not exist.

patch.summary:
UpdateExternalBackup modifies an existing external backup configuration
with optional stored credential reconciliation.
patch.description:
When update_password is false, credential fields in the request are
ignored and the stored credentials are preserved. When update_password
is true, credentials in the request replace stored credentials.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is empty, if validation fails,
or if update_password is false and the configuration does not exist.

get.summary:
GetExternalBackup returns the external backup configuration with the given
ID.
get.description:
Sensitive credential fields are replaced with a placeholder.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no configuration with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

delete.summary:
DeleteExternalBackup removes the external backup configuration with the
given ID and deregisters it from the integration health reporter.
delete.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

post.summary:
TriggerExternalBackup initiates an on-demand backup using the
configuration identified by the given ID.
post.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

put.summary:
PutExternalBackup replaces an existing external backup configuration,
treating all credential fields in the request as authoritative (no stored
credential reconciliation). Prefer UpdateExternalBackup for
credential-preserving updates.
put.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is empty or if validation fails.

get.summary: GetUpgradeStatus returns the current Central version and rollback capabilities.
get.description:
Reports the running version, the minimum DB version that can be safely rolled back to,
and whether rollback after the current upgrade is supported.

get.summary:
ListCloudSources returns all cloud sources matching the given filter,
sorted by name. Supports pagination via the pagination field (max 1000
results per page).

parameters.name: filter.names
parameters.description: Matches cloud sources based on their name.

parameters.name: filter.types
parameters.description:
Matches cloud sources based on their type.

 - TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD: TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD connects to the Paladin Cloud API for cluster discovery.
 - TYPE_OCM: TYPE_OCM connects to the Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager for cluster discovery.

post.summary: CreateCloudSource creates a new cloud source.
post.description:
The cloud_source.id field must be empty. Unless skip_test_integration is
true, the cloud source is tested for connectivity before being persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is set, if the cloud source is empty, or
if the connectivity test fails.

post.summary:
TestCloudSource verifies connectivity for the given cloud source
configuration using a short timeout (8 seconds) and no retries.
post.description:
When update_credentials is false, the stored credentials for the existing
cloud source are used. When update_credentials is true, the credentials in
the request are used directly.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cloud source is empty, if credential
reconciliation fails, or if the connectivity test fails.

put.summary: UpdateCloudSource replaces an existing cloud source configuration.
put.description:
When update_credentials is false, credentials in the request are ignored
and the stored credentials are used instead (the resource must already
exist). Unless skip_test_integration is true, connectivity is verified
before the update is persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cloud source is empty, if credential
reconciliation fails, or if the connectivity test fails.

parameters.name: cloudSource.id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source, assigned on creation.

get.summary: GetCloudSource retrieves a single cloud source by ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no cloud source with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source to retrieve.

delete.summary: DeleteCloudSource removes the cloud source with the given ID.
delete.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the cloud source to delete.

get.summary:
CountCloudSources returns the number of cloud sources matching the given
filter. Supports filtering by name or type.

parameters.name: filter.names
parameters.description: Matches cloud sources based on their name.

parameters.name: filter.types
parameters.description:
Matches cloud sources based on their type.

 - TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD: TYPE_PALADIN_CLOUD connects to the Paladin Cloud API for cluster discovery.
 - TYPE_OCM: TYPE_OCM connects to the Red Hat OpenShift Cluster Manager for cluster discovery.

get.summary: GetCAConfig returns Central's CA certificate rendered as a Helm values YAML bundle.
get.description: This can be used to configure trust for Central when deploying secured cluster components.

get.summary: GetCRSs returns metadata for all existing Cluster Registration Secrets.

post.summary:
GenerateCRS creates a new Cluster Registration Secret with the specified name and
returns the Kubernetes Secret manifest to apply in the secured cluster.
post.description:
Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if a CRS with the given name already exists.
Returns UNIMPLEMENTED if the CRS feature is not enabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CRSGenRequest is the request to generate a new Cluster Registration Secret with default settings.

post.summary:
GenerateCRSExtended creates a new Cluster Registration Secret with optional expiry and
registration limit constraints.
post.description:
Exactly one of valid_until or valid_for may be specified; providing both returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.
Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if a CRS with the given name already exists.
Returns UNIMPLEMENTED if the CRS feature is not enabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
CRSGenRequestExtended is the request to generate a new Cluster Registration Secret
with optional expiry and registration limits.

patch.summary: RevokeCRS revokes one or more Cluster Registration Secrets.
patch.description:
Revoked CRSs can no longer be used to register new clusters. Clusters that already completed
registration are not affected. Partial failures are reported per-ID in crs_revocation_errors.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CRSRevokeRequest is the request to revoke one or more Cluster Registration Secrets.

get.summary:
GetInitBundles returns metadata for all existing init bundles, including which clusters
are currently authenticated using each bundle.
Deprecated: use GetCRSs instead. Init bundles are superseded by Cluster Registration Secrets.

post.summary:
GenerateInitBundle creates a new init bundle with the specified name and returns its
credentials rendered as a Helm values YAML and as Kubernetes Secret manifests.
post.description:
Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if a bundle with the given name already exists.
Deprecated: use GenerateCRS instead.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
InitBundleGenRequest is the request to generate a new init bundle.
Deprecated: use CRSGenRequest instead.

patch.summary: RevokeInitBundle revokes one or more init bundles, preventing clusters from re-authenticating using them.
patch.description:
If any of the specified bundles are currently in use by clusters, the caller must include all
affected cluster IDs in confirm_impacted_clusters_ids. The request fails if any impacted cluster
is not confirmed, returning the list of impacted clusters in the error response.

Returns ALREADY_EXISTS if the revocation conflicts, or an error if confirmation is incomplete.
Deprecated: use RevokeCRS instead.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
InitBundleRevokeRequest is the request to revoke one or more init bundles.
Deprecated: use CRSRevokeRequest instead.

get.summary: GetClusterDefaultValues returns environment-level defaults used when creating a new Secured Cluster.
get.description:
Returns the default image repositories for the main StackRox component and Collector,
and whether kernel probe support (eBPF/CORE_BPF collection) is available from this Central.
These values are derived from Central's deployment configuration and probe source availability.

get.summary: GetClusters returns all Secured Clusters visible to the caller, optionally filtered by query.
get.description:
Clusters can be filtered using StackRox search query syntax (e.g. "Cluster:prod",
"Sensor Status:HEALTHY"). An empty query returns all clusters. The response includes
decommission retention info for any UNHEALTHY clusters if a retention policy is configured.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters clusters using StackRox search syntax.
Example: "Cluster:production" or "Cluster Status:UNHEALTHY".
If empty, all clusters visible to the caller are returned.

post.summary: PostCluster registers a new Secured Cluster with Central.
post.description:
The request body must be a storage.Cluster with the id field left empty; Central assigns
the ID upon creation. After creation, the cluster record must be used to generate Sensor
deployment manifests (Helm chart or kubectl manifests) which are applied to the target cluster.
Sensor then connects back to Central and begins populating health status and runtime data.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is non-empty.

get.summary: GetKernelSupportAvailable is deprecated in favor of GetClusterDefaultValues.

GetCluster returns the Secured Cluster with the given ID, including decommission retention info
if the cluster's sensor health is UNHEALTHY and a retention policy is configured.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no cluster with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

delete.summary:
DeleteCluster removes a Secured Cluster record from Central and initiates cleanup of
associated data (network graph, alerts, deployments, etc.) asynchronously.
delete.description:
This does NOT uninstall Sensor or other StackRox components from the target cluster.
Sensor will attempt to reconnect after deletion and the cluster can be re-registered.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is empty.

put.summary: PutCluster updates an existing Secured Cluster record.
put.description:
The request body must be a storage.Cluster with a non-empty id field identifying the cluster
to update. Dynamic configuration fields (e.g. admission controller config, registry override)
are propagated to Sensor over the gRPC connection without requiring a Sensor restart. Static
configuration changes (e.g. collection method, image references) take effect on next Sensor restart.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is empty.

get.summary: GetRecentRuns returns compliance runs matching the optional cluster, standard, and time filters.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id restricts results to runs for the specified cluster.

parameters.name: standardId
parameters.description: standard_id restricts results to runs for the specified compliance standard.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: since filters runs to those that started at or after this timestamp.

post.summary: TriggerRuns starts new compliance runs for the specified cluster/standard selection.
post.description:
The selection supports wildcard values ("*") to trigger runs across all clusters
or all standards. Returns the list of ComplianceRun objects that were started.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cluster or standard selection cannot be resolved.
Requires write access to the Compliance resource.

get.summary: GetRunStatuses returns the current status for specified run IDs or the latest runs.
get.description:
Set latest=true to retrieve the most recent run per cluster/standard pair without
specifying IDs. Setting both latest=true and run_ids returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.
IDs in the request that do not match any run are returned in invalid_run_ids.

Requires read access to the Compliance resource.

parameters.name: runIds
parameters.description: run_ids is the list of compliance run IDs to query. Mutually exclusive with latest.

parameters.name: latest
parameters.description:
latest when true returns the most recent run for each cluster/standard pair,
ignoring run_ids. Cannot be combined with run_ids.

get.summary:
GetAggregatedResults returns compliance check pass/fail counts aggregated
across the fleet according to the requested grouping and granularity.
get.description:
The `unit` field controls granularity (what each result row counts):
CHECK gives one row per individual check, CONTROL per control, etc.
The `group_by` field adds breakdown dimensions such as CLUSTER, STANDARD,
NAMESPACE, NODE, DEPLOYMENT, CATEGORY, or CONTROL.
The `where` field filters results using StackRox search query syntax
(e.g. "Cluster:production+Standard:CIS").

Standards with hideScanResults=true are excluded from the response.
Requires View permission on the Compliance resource.

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description:
group_by adds breakdown dimensions to the aggregation result.
Each scope in this list becomes an additional key in every result row.
Example: [CLUSTER, STANDARD] groups results by cluster and standard.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.description:
unit specifies the granularity of each result row — the entity type that
num_passing, num_failing, and num_skipped counts refer to.
Defaults to CHECK when unset or UNKNOWN.

parameters.name: where.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetRunResults returns the compliance scan results for a specific cluster
and standard.
get.description:
By default, returns the most recent successful run's results along with
metadata for any failed runs. Specify run_id to retrieve results for a
specific historical run; note that bypassing the cache may increase
memory pressure on the server.

Requires View permission on the Compliance resource.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the ID of the secured cluster whose results to return.

parameters.name: standardId
parameters.description:
standard_id is the compliance standard whose results to return
(e.g. "CIS_Kubernetes_v1_5_0").

parameters.name: runId
parameters.description:
Specifies the run ID for which to return results. If empty, the most recent run is returned.
CAVEAT: Setting this field circumvents the results cache on the server-side, which may lead to significantly
        increased memory pressure and decreased performance.

get.summary:
GetStandards returns the list of compliance standards active on at least
one secured cluster.
get.description:
Only standards that the compliance operator manager considers active are
returned. For each standard, the hideScanResults flag reflects the current
configuration set via UpdateComplianceStandardConfig.

Requires View permission on the Compliance resource.

get.summary:
GetStandard returns the full definition of a compliance standard by ID,
including all control groups and individual controls.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if the standard ID does not exist.
Requires View permission on the Compliance resource.

patch.summary:
UpdateComplianceStandardConfig updates display configuration for a
compliance standard, currently limited to the hideScanResults flag.
patch.description:
When hideScanResults is true, the standard's results are suppressed in
GetAggregatedResults responses. The standard itself remains visible via
GetStandards and GetStandard.

Returns NOT_FOUND if the standard ID does not exist.
Requires Modify permission on the Compliance resource.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the compliance standard ID to update (e.g. the standard's short name).

get.summary:
GetConfig returns the complete Central configuration, including public config, private config,
and platform component config.
get.description: Requires View on Administration.

put.summary: PutConfig replaces the entire Central configuration in a single write.
put.description:
Both public_config and private_config must be present in the request; omitting either
returns INVALID_ARGUMENT. Platform component rules (if provided) are validated for
non-empty names and valid RE2 regex patterns. The vulnerability exception config embedded
in private_config is also validated.

Side effects on success:
  - Telemetry collection is enabled or disabled based on public_config.telemetry.enabled.
  - Platform component classification is re-evaluated for all workloads asynchronously.
  - Custom Prometheus metrics aggregation is reconfigured.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if required sub-configs are missing or any validation fails.
Requires Modify on Administration.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: PutConfigRequest carries the full Central configuration to replace in a single write.

get.summary: GetPlatformComponentConfig returns the current platform component classification rules.
get.description:
Platform component rules are namespace-matching regexes used to label workloads as
platform (infrastructure) rather than user workloads. This endpoint is only available
when the CustomizablePlatformComponents feature flag is enabled; returns an error otherwise.

Requires View on Administration.

put.summary: UpdatePlatformComponentConfig replaces the platform component namespace-matching rules.
put.description:
Each rule must have a non-empty name and a valid RE2 regex for namespace matching.
After a successful update, platform component classification is re-evaluated for all
known workloads asynchronously.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any rule has an empty name, empty regex, or an invalid regex pattern.
Returns an error if the CustomizablePlatformComponents feature flag is disabled.
Requires Modify on Administration.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
PutPlatformComponentConfigRequest carries the namespace-matching rules used to classify
workloads as platform components rather than user workloads.

get.summary: GetDefaultRedHatLayeredProductsRegex returns a static string containing the default Red Hat Layered Products regex.
get.description:
This regex is the built-in pattern used to identify namespaces belonging to Red Hat layered
products (e.g. OpenShift operators). It can be used as a starting point when configuring
custom platform component rules via UpdatePlatformComponentConfig.

Requires View on Administration.

get.summary: GetPrivateConfig returns the private (admin-only) portion of Central's configuration.
get.description:
Private config contains all data-retention settings: alert retention durations (by type),
image scan retention, expired vulnerability request retention, decommissioned cluster
retention, report history and downloadable-report retention, administration event retention,
and custom Prometheus metrics configuration.

Requires View on the Administration resource.

get.summary:
GetVulnerabilityExceptionConfig returns the current vulnerability exception (deferral/false-positive)
configuration, which controls which expiry options are offered when users create exception requests.
get.description: Requires View on VulnerabilityManagementRequests OR View on Administration.

put.summary: UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionConfig replaces the vulnerability exception configuration.
put.description:
Validates that:
  - at least one DayOption is provided and at least one expiry option is enabled overall,
  - all enabled DayOptions have unique num_days values >= 1,
  - fixable_cve_options is present.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Requires Modify on Administration.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionConfigRequest carries the desired vulnerability exception
configuration to store. All expiry_options validation rules apply.

get.summary: GetPublicConfig returns the public portion of Central's configuration.
get.description:
Public config includes UI banners (header/footer), the login-page notice, and the
telemetry opt-in status. This endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated so that banners
can be displayed on the login page before the user has a session.

get.summary: GetCertExpiry returns information related to the expiry component mTLS certificate.

patch.summary:
SuppressCVEs snoozes the given cluster-infrastructure CVEs for the specified
duration.
patch.description:
Suppression sets the CVE state to snoozed, hiding it from vulnerability
management views until the snooze duration elapses or UnsuppressCVEs is
called. Because cluster CVEs are not part of the policy workflow, suppression
does not affect policy violations or risk reprocessing.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if no CVE identifiers are provided.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the VulnMgmtLegacySnooze feature flag is disabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: SuppressCVERequest is the request message for suppressing (snoozeing) one or more CVEs.

patch.summary:
UnsuppressCVEs lifts the snooze from the given cluster-infrastructure CVEs,
returning them to the OBSERVED state immediately regardless of any remaining
snooze duration.
patch.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if no CVE identifiers are provided.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the VulnMgmtLegacySnooze feature flag is disabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: UnsuppressCVERequest is the request message for lifting suppression from one or more CVEs.

patch.summary: SuppressCVEs snoozes the given node CVEs for the specified duration.
patch.description:
Suppression sets the CVE state to snoozed, hiding it from vulnerability
management views until the snooze duration elapses or UnsuppressCVEs is
called. Because nodes are not part of the policy workflow, suppression does
not affect policy violations or risk reprocessing.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if no CVE identifiers are provided.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the VulnMgmtLegacySnooze feature flag is disabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: SuppressCVERequest is the request message for suppressing (snoozeing) one or more CVEs.

patch.summary:
UnsuppressCVEs lifts the snooze from the given node CVEs, returning them to
the OBSERVED state immediately regardless of any remaining snooze duration.
patch.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if no CVE identifiers are provided.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the VulnMgmtLegacySnooze feature flag is disabled.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: UnsuppressCVERequest is the request message for lifting suppression from one or more CVEs.

get.summary:
GetExportCapabilities returns the database export formats and encoding
types supported by this Central instance. Used to determine which format
to request when initiating a restore.

post.summary:
InterruptRestoreProcess pauses the active restore process at the current
byte position, allowing it to be resumed later.
post.description:
Returns the byte offset (resume_info) at which the restore was paused.
Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if no restore is active or if the provided
process_id does not match the active process.

parameters.name: processId
parameters.description: process_id identifies the restore process to interrupt.

parameters.name: attemptId
parameters.description: attempt_id identifies the specific attempt to interrupt.

get.summary:
GetActiveRestoreProcess returns the status of the currently active
database restore process, if any. Returns an empty response if no restore
is currently in progress.

delete.summary:
CancelRestoreProcess permanently cancels the active database restore
process identified by the given ID and waits for it to terminate.
delete.description:
Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if no restore is active or if the provided
ID does not match the active process.

get.summary: Stream authorization traces for all incoming requests.

post.summary: Reset database debugging statistics.

get.summary: Get the current logging level for StackRox services.

patch.summary: Set logging level for StackRox services.

get.summary:
GetDeclarativeConfigHealths returns the health status of all declaratively
configured resources, including any errors encountered during the last
reconciliation pass.
GetConfig returns the current delegated registry configuration

put.summary: UpdateConfig updates the stored delegated registry configuration

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
DelegatedRegistryConfig determines if and where scan requests are delegated to, such as kept in
central services or sent to particular secured clusters.

get.summary:
GetClusters returns the list of clusters (id + name) and a flag indicating whether or not
the cluster is valid for use in the delegated registry config

ListDeployments returns a paginated list of slim deployment summaries
matching the query.
get.description:
Each result is a storage.ListDeployment containing only identity and summary
fields (id, name, cluster, namespace, created, priority). Use GetDeployment
to retrieve the full object for a specific deployment.

Results are capped at 1000 deployments per request. Use pagination to page
through larger result sets. Supports sorting via the pagination.sortOption
field (e.g., by "Deployment Risk Priority").

Uses StackRox search query syntax. Searchable fields include Cluster,
Namespace, Deployment, Deployment Type, and Deployment Label.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetLabels returns all label keys and their observed values across all
deployments visible to the caller.
get.description:
The response contains a map of label key to sorted list of distinct values,
plus a flat sorted list of all values across all keys. This is typically
used to populate filter dropdowns in the UI.

No query filtering is applied; all deployment labels are always returned.

get.summary: GetDeployment returns the full deployment object with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no deployment with the given ID exists within the
caller's authorized scope.

get.summary: CountDeployments returns the number of deployments that match the query.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search query syntax. If the query is empty, counts all
deployments visible to the caller.
Example query: "Cluster:production+Namespace:payments"

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
ListDeploymentsWithProcessInfo returns a paginated list of slim deployment
summaries, each paired with per-container process baseline evaluation results.
get.description:
Applies the same query, pagination, and 1000-result cap as ListDeployments.

Baseline statuses indicate whether each container's process baseline is
locked, unlocked, or not yet generated, and whether anomalous processes have
been observed. Baseline statuses are only populated when the caller has
read access to the DeploymentExtension resource in addition to Deployment.
If the caller lacks DeploymentExtension access, deployments are still
returned but baseline_statuses will be empty on each entry.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetDeploymentWithRisk returns the full deployment object together with its
computed risk score and the individual factors that contributed to it.
get.description:
Risk is calculated by the StackRox risk engine and reflects security
posture signals such as image vulnerabilities, exposed ports, and
privileged container settings.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no deployment with the given ID exists within the
caller's authorized scope.

get.summary:
ExportDeployments streams full deployment objects matching the query as a
server-sent stream of ExportDeploymentResponse messages.
get.description:
Unlike ListDeployments, this endpoint streams full storage.Deployment objects
(not slim summaries) and has no upper bound on the number of results. It is
intended for bulk data export scenarios.

An optional timeout (in seconds) can be set on the request; if the stream
does not complete within the timeout, it is cancelled.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description:
timeout is an optional deadline in seconds for the streaming export.
If zero, no deadline is applied. If positive, the stream will be
cancelled after this many seconds.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters exported deployments using StackRox search syntax.
If empty, all deployments visible to the caller are exported.
Example: "Cluster:production+Namespace:payments"

post.summary: DetectBuildTime checks a container image against all active build-time policies.
post.description:
The image can be specified as a full storage.ContainerImage object or as an image name string.
If a cluster is specified, the image scan is delegated to that cluster's scanner using the
cluster's pull secrets to access private registries.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if neither image nor image_name is specified, if force and
no_external_metadata are both set, or if a specified policy_category does not match any policy.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: BuildDetectionRequest is the request message for DetectBuildTime.

post.summary: DetectDeployTime checks a single deployment object against all active deploy-time policies.
post.description:
Enriches the deployment's container images with vulnerability data before running detection.
When enforcement_only is true, only evaluates policies with deploy-time enforcement actions
(scale-to-zero or unsatisfiable node constraint); returns empty results if no such policies exist.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the deployment is nil or if the specified cluster_id does not exist.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: DeployDetectionRequest is the request message for DetectDeployTime.

post.summary: DetectDeployTimeFromYAML checks Kubernetes workload manifests in YAML format against deploy-time policies.
post.description:
Parses one or more Kubernetes resource manifests from the yaml field (multi-document YAML supported).
Non-deployment resources (e.g. ConfigMaps) and unrecognized resource types are skipped and reported
in ignored_object_refs. When a cluster is specified, enriches deployments with live cluster data
(service account permissions, applied network policies) via the connected Sensor.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the yaml field is empty, if the YAML cannot be parsed, or if every
deployment in the YAML fails to parse. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the specified cluster is not found
or its Sensor connection is not ready.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: DeployYAMLDetectionRequest is the request message for DetectDeployTimeFromYAML.

get.summary: CountDiscoveredClusters returns the number of discovered clusters after filtering by requested fields.

parameters.name: filter.names
parameters.description: Matches discovered clusters of specific names.

parameters.name: filter.types
parameters.description:
Matches discovered clusters of specific types.

 - AKS: AKS is Azure Kubernetes Service.
 - ARO: ARO is Azure Red Hat OpenShift.
 - EKS: EKS is Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service.
 - GKE: GKE is Google Kubernetes Engine.
 - OCP: OCP is OpenShift Container Platform (self-managed).
 - OSD: OSD is OpenShift Dedicated.
 - ROSA: ROSA is Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS.

parameters.name: filter.statuses
parameters.description:
Matches discovered clusters of specific statuses.

 - STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: The status of the cluster is unknown. May occur if a secured cluster
is missing the metadata for a possible match.
 - STATUS_SECURED: The discovered cluster was matched with a secured cluster.
 - STATUS_UNSECURED: The discovered cluster was not matched with a secured cluster.

parameters.name: filter.sourceIds
parameters.description: Matches discovered clusters of specific cloud source IDs.

get.summary: ListDiscoveredClusters returns the list of discovered clusters after filtered by requested fields.
get.description:
Results are sorted by cluster name ascending. Pagination is supported with a maximum of 1000
results per page.

parameters.name: filter.names
parameters.description: Matches discovered clusters of specific names.

parameters.name: filter.types
parameters.description:
Matches discovered clusters of specific types.

 - AKS: AKS is Azure Kubernetes Service.
 - ARO: ARO is Azure Red Hat OpenShift.
 - EKS: EKS is Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service.
 - GKE: GKE is Google Kubernetes Engine.
 - OCP: OCP is OpenShift Container Platform (self-managed).
 - OSD: OSD is OpenShift Dedicated.
 - ROSA: ROSA is Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS.

parameters.name: filter.statuses
parameters.description:
Matches discovered clusters of specific statuses.

 - STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: The status of the cluster is unknown. May occur if a secured cluster
is missing the metadata for a possible match.
 - STATUS_SECURED: The discovered cluster was matched with a secured cluster.
 - STATUS_UNSECURED: The discovered cluster was not matched with a secured cluster.

parameters.name: filter.sourceIds
parameters.description: Matches discovered clusters of specific cloud source IDs.

get.summary: GetDiscoveredCluster retrieves a discovered cluster by ID.
get.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no discovered cluster exists with the given ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUIDv5 identifier of the discovered cluster to retrieve.

get.summary: GetFeatureFlags returns all known feature flags and their current enabled state.
get.description:
The response is sorted alphabetically by flag name. This endpoint is anonymous
(no authentication required) and is used by the UI at startup.

get.summary: GetGroup returns the single group matching the given group properties.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no group matches the specified properties.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for group properties and respectively the group.

parameters.name: traits.expiresAt
parameters.description:
expires_at specifies when this object should be considered expired and eligible for pruning.
This field is optional. Objects without an expires_at value are never pruned based on expiry.
Currently used for dynamically created RBAC objects (roles, permission sets, access scopes)
that are generated by the internal token API for sensors.

get.summary:
GetGroups returns all groups, optionally filtered by auth provider ID, attribute key,
attribute value, and/or group ID.
get.description:
All specified filters are ANDed. Omitting all filters returns all groups.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: authProviderId
parameters.description: auth_provider_id filters groups to those belonging to the specified auth provider.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: key filters groups by the attribute key used for membership matching (e.g. "groups").

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: value filters groups by the attribute value used for membership matching.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id filters groups by their unique group ID.

delete.summary: DeleteGroup removes the group identified by the given properties.
delete.description: Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: authProviderId
parameters.description:
We copy over parameters from storage.GroupProperties for seamless HTTP API migration.
auth_provider_id identifies the auth provider the group belongs to.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: key is the attribute key of the group properties to match.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: value is the attribute value of the group properties to match.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the group to delete.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: force bypasses immutability checks enforced by object traits (e.g. declarative-origin groups).

post.summary: CreateGroup creates a new group mapping an auth provider attribute triple to a role.
post.description: Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Group is a GroupProperties : Role mapping.

put.summary: UpdateGroup replaces the role assigned by an existing group.
put.description:
The group.props.id must be set and must match an existing group.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails or the group does not exist.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: group is the updated group definition. The group ID must be set and must match an existing group.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: force bypasses immutability checks enforced by object traits (e.g. declarative-origin groups).

post.summary: BatchUpdate applies an atomic diff between previous_groups and required_groups in a single transaction.
post.description:
Groups in previous_groups but not required_groups are deleted.
Groups in both are updated if they differ.
Groups in required_groups without an ID are added (duplicates by (props, role) are deduplicated).
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any group fails validation.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

get.summary:
Get returns the gRPC preferences for this Central instance, including the
maximum receive message size in bytes.

GetImageIntegrations returns all image integrations, optionally filtered
by name.
get.description:
Sensitive credential fields are scrubbed from the response for all callers
except Sensor service accounts. Results can be filtered by exact name match
using GetImageIntegrationsRequest.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: name filters the results to integrations whose name exactly matches this value.

parameters.name: cluster
parameters.description: cluster is reserved for future use and is currently ignored.

post.summary: PostImageIntegration creates a new image integration.
post.description:
The id field must be empty; a new ID is assigned and returned. The
integration is validated and tested (unless skip_test_integration is set)
before it is persisted. On success, connected Sensors are notified.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is set, if required fields are
missing, if validation fails, or if the integration test fails.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the type is "scanner_v4" (auto-managed).

post.summary:
TestImageIntegration verifies that the given image integration
configuration is reachable and correctly set up, treating all credential
fields as authoritative (no stored credential reconciliation).
post.description:
Tests each configured category (registry, scanner, node scanner)
individually.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any test fails or validation fails.

post.summary:
TestUpdatedImageIntegration verifies that the given image integration
configuration is reachable with optional stored credential reconciliation.
post.description:
When updatePassword is false, credential fields in the request are replaced
with the stored credentials before the test is performed. When
updatePassword is true, the provided credentials are used directly.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any test fails or validation fails.
Returns NOT_FOUND if updatePassword is false and the integration does not
exist.

patch.summary:
UpdateImageIntegration modifies an existing image integration with
optional stored credential reconciliation.
patch.description:
When updatePassword is false, credential fields in the request are ignored
and the stored credentials are preserved. When updatePassword is true,
credentials in the request replace stored credentials. On success,
connected Sensors are notified of the change.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation or integration testing fails.
Returns NOT_FOUND if updatePassword is false and the integration does not
exist.

get.summary: GetImageIntegration returns the image integration with the given ID.
get.description:
Sensitive credential fields are replaced with a placeholder in the
response.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no integration with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

delete.summary: DeleteImageIntegration removes the image integration with the given ID.
delete.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the integration is of type "scanner_v4"
(auto-managed integrations cannot be deleted).
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

put.summary:
PutImageIntegration replaces an existing image integration, treating all
credential fields in the request as authoritative (no stored credential
reconciliation). Prefer UpdateImageIntegration for credential-preserving
updates.
put.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation or integration testing fails.

get.summary: ExportImages streams all images matching the query as a sequence of full image objects.
get.description:
Each response message in the stream contains one fully-enriched storage.Image.
This endpoint is intended for bulk export use cases where the full vulnerability
detail of every matching image is needed. Use ListImages for interactive queries.

An optional timeout (in seconds) can be set; the stream will be canceled if the
export takes longer than the specified duration.

Requires View Image permission.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description:
timeout is the maximum number of seconds the export may run before being canceled.
A value of 0 means no timeout.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters the exported images using StackRox search query syntax.
An empty query exports all images.

get.summary: ListImages returns lightweight image summaries matching the query.
get.description:
Returns storage.ListImage objects (slim form) which include name, scan time,
component count, CVE count, and risk score, but omit per-component vulnerability
details. Use GetImage to retrieve the full scan data for a specific image.

Supports StackRox search query syntax and pagination. Results are capped at
1000 images per request.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

delete.summary:
DeleteImages removes images matching the query from Central's datastore and
broadcasts an invalidation message to all connected Sensors.
delete.description:
The request requires a non-nil query; an empty query targets all images.
When confirm=false the response reports the number of images that would be
deleted without making any changes (dry run). Set confirm=true to perform
the actual deletion.

Requires Modify Image permission.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if no query is provided or the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description:
confirm must be set to true to perform the actual deletion.
If false, the response reflects the number of images that would be deleted
without making any changes (dry run).

get.summary:
InvalidateScanAndRegistryCaches clears the in-memory image metadata cache used to
avoid redundant registry and scanner calls.
get.description:
After invalidation, the next scan or enrichment request for any image will re-fetch
metadata from the registry and re-run the scanner. This is useful after adding or
updating image integrations.

Requires Modify Image permission.

post.summary: ScanImage triggers an on-demand scan of the named image and returns the full result.
post.description:
Central contacts the configured image registry to fetch metadata, then runs the
image through the configured scanner (Clairify or Scanner V4) to produce vulnerability
data. The enriched image is persisted to Central's datastore.

By default the scan may be served from the metadata cache; set force=true to bypass
the cache and fetch fresh data from the registry and scanner. The scan can be
delegated to a specific secured cluster by providing the cluster name or ID.

Requires Modify Image permission.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the image name cannot be resolved.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: ScanImageRequest is the request message for ScanImage.

get.summary:
GetImage returns the full image record for the given SHA digest, including all
per-component vulnerability details.
get.description:
Unlike ListImages (which returns slim objects), GetImage returns the complete
storage.Image including the embedded scan with all components and CVEs.
Snoozed CVEs are excluded by default; set include_snoozed=true to include them.
CVE descriptions are included by default; set strip_description=true to omit them.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no image with the given SHA exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the image SHA (digest). Required.

parameters.name: includeSnoozed
parameters.description:
include_snoozed controls whether CVEs that have been snoozed (suppressed)
are included in the returned scan data. Defaults to false (snoozed CVEs are excluded).

parameters.name: stripDescription
parameters.description:
strip_description removes verbose CVE description text from the response,
reducing payload size when description details are not needed.

get.summary: CountImages returns the number of images matching the query.
get.description:
Accepts StackRox search query syntax (e.g. "Image:nginx+CVE:CVE-2021-44228").
An empty query counts all images visible to the caller.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetWatchedImages returns the list of image names that are currently
being watched.
get.description: Requires View WatchedImage permission.

delete.summary: UnwatchImage removes an image name from the watched list.
delete.description:
Returns successfully if the image is no longer being watched
after the call, irrespective of whether the image was already being watched.

Requires Modify WatchedImage permission.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
The name of the image to unwatch.
Should match the name of a previously watched image.

post.summary:
WatchImage registers an image name for continuous monitoring and triggers an
immediate scan.
post.description:
Central will periodically re-scan watched images so that vulnerability data
stays current even when no deployment currently references them. The image name
must be fully qualified including a tag but must NOT include a digest (SHA).

The response always returns HTTP 200; scan failure is signaled via the error_type
and error_message fields rather than an HTTP error code.

Requires Modify WatchedImage permission.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: WatchImageRequest is the request message for WatchImage.

post.summary: Generate an SPDX 2.3 SBOM from an image scan.

get.summary:
GetDeclarativeConfigs returns the health status for all declarative
configuration sources (e.g. config maps used for GitOps-style management).

GetBackupPlugins returns the health status for all configured external
backup integrations.

GetImageIntegrations returns the health status for all configured image
registry and scanner integrations.

GetNotifiers returns the health status for all configured notifier
integrations (e.g. Slack, email, PagerDuty).

GetVulnDefinitionsInfo returns metadata about the vulnerability definitions
database for the specified scanner component (Scanner or Scanner V4),
including the timestamp of the last successful update.

parameters.name: component
parameters.description:
component selects which scanner's vulnerability definition metadata to return.

 - SCANNER: SCANNER refers to the legacy StackRox Scanner (Clairify).
 - SCANNER_V4: SCANNER_V4 refers to the StackRox Scanner V4.

get.summary: GetDatabaseBackupStatus returns information about the most recent database backup recorded by Central.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if no backup has been performed or recorded yet.

get.summary: GetCentralCapabilities returns which optional Central features are available in the current deployment.
get.description:
Capabilities may be disabled for managed (cloud-hosted) Central deployments where certain
self-managed integrations or operations are not applicable.

get.summary: GetDatabaseStatus reports whether Central can reach its database, and the database type and version.
get.description:
Database type and version are only returned to authenticated callers. The database_available
field is always populated and can be used as a readiness indicator.

get.summary: GetMetadata returns version and build information about Central.
get.description:
The version field is only populated for authenticated callers; anonymous requests receive
build_flavor and release_build only.

get.summary:
TLSChallenge returns Central's trusted CA certificates and leaf certificate, signed with
Central's internal key, to allow Sensor to verify Central's identity without pre-shared trust.
get.description:
This endpoint is unauthenticated to support Sensor bootstrap scenarios where Central is behind
a load balancer with self-signed certificates or during CA rotation. The challenge_token
(base64-encoded, fixed-length random bytes) prevents replay attacks.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if challenge_token is not valid base64 or has an incorrect length.

parameters.name: challengeToken
parameters.description:
challenge_token is a base64-encoded random token generated by the caller (Sensor).
Must decode to exactly the expected number of bytes to prevent replay attacks.

get.summary: ListMitreAttackVectors returns all MITRE ATT&CK vectors.
get.description:
Each vector represents a tactic (e.g. "Initial Access") and includes all its associated
techniques. The response is approximately 1 MB. Requires an authenticated session.

get.summary: GetMitreAttackVector returns the full MITRE ATT&CK vector for a tactic with all its techniques.
get.description:
The id field must be a valid MITRE ATT&CK tactic ID (e.g. "TA0001").
Returns NOT_FOUND if no tactic with the given ID exists in the local knowledge base.

get.summary: GetNamespaces returns a list of namespaces matching the optional query filter.
get.description:
Supports StackRox search query syntax to filter by cluster, namespace name, or label.
Returns up to MaxInt32 results. Use the pagination field in the query for paging.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetNamespace returns the namespace with the given ID, enriched with deployment,
secret, and network policy counts.
get.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty or the namespace is not found.

patch.summary:
ModifyBaselineStatusForPeers adds or removes peer connections from a
deployment's network baseline.
patch.description:
Peers with status BASELINE are added to the baseline; peers with status
ANOMALOUS are removed. Changes are persisted and propagated to Sensor so
that new observations are classified correctly.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment whose baseline to modify. Required.

post.summary:
GetNetworkBaselineStatusForFlows classifies a batch of peer connections
as BASELINE or ANOMALOUS relative to the given deployment's network baseline.
post.description:
If no baseline exists for the deployment, one is created automatically
before classification. Discovered external peers (e.g. dynamic CIDR blocks)
are anonymized to the INTERNET entity for comparison purposes.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment whose baseline to check against. Required.

get.summary:
GetNetworkBaselineStatusForExternalFlows returns the baseline status of
observed external network flows for a deployment, paginated and split into
anomalous and baseline groups.
get.description:
Flows are fetched from the network graph for the given time window (default:
last 1 hour). Results are sorted by peer entity name before pagination.

If no baseline exists for the deployment, one is created automatically.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment to query. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query additionally filters external entities using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 1 hour before the current time if unset.

get.summary: GetNetworkBaseline returns the network baseline for the given deployment ID.
get.description:
If no baseline exists, one is created automatically. The baseline contains
all peer connections (port, protocol, direction) currently marked as expected.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the deployment ID is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist and a baseline cannot
be created.

patch.summary:
LockNetworkBaseline locks a deployment's network baseline to prevent
further automatic updates.
patch.description:
Once locked, new observed connections are not automatically added to the
baseline and will be classified as anomalous. The baseline can still be
modified manually via ModifyBaselineStatusForPeers.

patch.summary:
UnlockNetworkBaseline unlocks a deployment's network baseline, allowing
new observed connections to be added automatically.

get.summary:
GetNetworkGraph returns a snapshot of observed network flows for all
deployments in the given cluster.
get.description:
Flows are filtered to those observed after the `since` timestamp (default:
5 minutes ago). The result graph includes deployment nodes, external source
nodes, and directed edges with per-port connection metadata when
`include_ports` is true.

Deployments invisible to the caller due to Scoped Access Control are masked
(replaced with anonymized nodes) rather than omitted, to preserve graph
topology. Flows between two invisible deployments are omitted entirely.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty or the number of matching
deployments exceeds the configured maximum.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist or is not visible.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the ID of the cluster to retrieve the network graph for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters deployments shown in the graph using StackRox search syntax.
Example: "Namespace:production+Label:app=frontend"

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 5 minutes before the current time if unset.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description:
include_ports controls whether per-port connection details are included in
edge properties.

parameters.name: scope.query
parameters.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

parameters.name: includePolicies
parameters.description:
include_policies controls whether NetworkPolicy isolation information
(non_isolated_ingress, non_isolated_egress, policy_ids) is populated on
each deployment node.

get.summary:
GetExternalNetworkEntities returns the user-defined external network
entities (CIDR blocks) visible for the given cluster, optionally filtered
by the search query.
get.description:
Results include both cluster-scoped entities and global (cluster-agnostic)
entities. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query is malformed.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id restricts results to entities scoped to this cluster (or global entities).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query further filters entities using StackRox search syntax.

post.summary:
CreateExternalNetworkEntity creates a new user-defined external network
entity (CIDR block) scoped to the given cluster.
post.description:
The entity ID is derived deterministically from the cluster ID and CIDR
block. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cluster ID or CIDR is invalid.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist or is not visible.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to which this external entity is scoped. Required.

get.summary:
GetExternalNetworkFlowsMetadata returns per-entity flow counts for all
external network entities that have observed flows in the cluster.
get.description:
Results are sorted by entity ID and support pagination. Useful for
building summary views without fetching full flow details.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to query. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters deployments using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 5 minutes before the current time if unset.

get.summary:
GetExternalNetworkFlows returns the observed network flows between cluster
deployments and a specific external network entity.
get.description:
Flows are paginated and sorted by source entity ID for deterministic
pagination. Only flows involving at least one deployment visible to the
caller are returned.

Returns NOT_FOUND if the external entity does not exist.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to query for external flows. Required.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: entity_id is the ID of the external network entity to retrieve flows for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query additionally filters the set of deployments considered, using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
since restricts flows to those observed after this timestamp.
Defaults to 5 minutes before the current time if unset.

get.summary: GetNetworkGraphConfig returns the current network graph display configuration.
get.description:
The configuration controls settings such as whether default external
network sources are hidden in the graph. Requires View access to
the Administration resource.

put.summary: PutNetworkGraphConfig replaces the network graph display configuration.
put.description:
