Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the config body is missing.
Requires Modify access to the Administration resource.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: PutNetworkGraphConfigRequest wraps the new network graph configuration to persist.

delete.summary: DeleteExternalNetworkEntity removes a user-defined external network entity.
delete.description:
Only user-created entities can be deleted; StackRox-generated default
entities are immutable and will return PERMISSION_DENIED.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the entity does not exist.

patch.summary:
PatchExternalNetworkEntity renames an existing user-defined external
network entity.
patch.description:
Only user-created entities can be updated; StackRox-generated default
entities are immutable and will return PERMISSION_DENIED.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the entity ID is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the entity does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the ID of the external network entity to update. Required.

get.summary:
GetNetworkPolicies returns all NetworkPolicies in a cluster, optionally
filtered by namespace or by the set of deployments they apply to.
get.description:
When deployment_query is provided, only policies that are selected by at
least one matching deployment are returned. YAML is populated for all
returned policies.

Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id restricts results to policies in this cluster. Required.

parameters.name: deploymentQuery
parameters.description:
deployment_query is an optional StackRox search query; when set, only
policies that apply to at least one matching deployment are returned.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: namespace restricts results to policies in this Kubernetes namespace.

get.summary:
GetAllowedPeersFromCurrentPolicyForDeployment returns all peers
(deployments and external entities) that are currently permitted to
communicate with the given deployment based on active NetworkPolicies.
get.description:
Each peer entry includes the port, protocol, and direction (ingress/egress).
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the deployment is not found.

post.summary:
ApplyNetworkPolicyYamlForDeployment applies a network policy modification
to the cluster of the specified deployment and records an undo record keyed
by deployment ID.
post.description:
Behaves like ApplyNetworkPolicy but stores the undo record per deployment
rather than per cluster.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist.
Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if no active Sensor connection exists for the cluster.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is used to look up the target cluster and to key the undo record.

post.summary:
ApplyNetworkPolicy applies a NetworkPolicy modification to a cluster via
the connected Sensor and stores an undo record.
post.description:
The modification must be non-empty and all policies must have non-empty
namespaces. Changes to the stackrox and kube-system namespaces are
forbidden and will return INVALID_ARGUMENT. The caller must have write
access to all namespaces referenced in the modification.

Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if no active Sensor connection exists for the cluster.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to apply the modification to. Required.

parameters.name: modification
parameters.description: modification describes the policies to create/update and the policies to delete.

get.summary:
GetDiffFlowsBetweenPolicyAndBaselineForDeployment compares the peers
currently allowed by active NetworkPolicies against those that would be
allowed by the baseline-generated NetworkPolicy for the deployment.
get.description:
Returns added (newly allowed), removed (no longer allowed), and reconciled
(present in both, with per-property diffs) peer groups.

get.summary:
GetNetworkGraph returns a policy-based network connectivity graph for a
cluster, showing which deployments are permitted to communicate based on
the active NetworkPolicies (not observed flows).
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist or is not visible.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to generate the graph for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters deployments shown as query-match nodes using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description: If set to true, include port-level information in the network policy graph.

parameters.name: scope.query
parameters.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

post.summary:
GetBaselineGeneratedNetworkPolicyForDeployment generates a NetworkPolicy
for a specific deployment based on its network baseline (the set of
connections marked as expected).
post.description:
The result is a NetworkPolicyModification ready to be previewed or applied.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_id is empty.
Requires View access to NetworkPolicy and DeploymentExtension.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment to generate the policy for. Required.

get.summary:
GenerateNetworkPolicies auto-generates Kubernetes NetworkPolicies for
deployments in a cluster based on observed network flows.
get.description:
The result is a NetworkPolicyModification containing YAML for the generated
policies and references to existing policies to delete (per delete_existing
mode). Flows are restricted to those after network_data_since if set.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty.
Requires View access to NetworkPolicy and NetworkGraph.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to generate policies for. Required.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query filters the deployments whose observed flows are used for generation,
using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: deleteExisting
parameters.description:
delete_existing controls whether existing policies are deleted before applying
the generated ones. Defaults to NONE if UNKNOWN.

 - NONE: Do not delete any existing network policies.
 - GENERATED_ONLY: Delete any existing *auto-generated* network policies.
 - ALL: Delete all existing network policies in the respective namespace.

parameters.name: networkDataSince
parameters.description:
network_data_since restricts flows used for generation to those observed
after this timestamp.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description:
include_ports controls whether per-port connection details are reflected in
the generated policy rules.

get.summary:
GetNetworkGraphEpoch returns the current epoch counter for the network
policy graph of a cluster.
get.description:
The epoch increments whenever the network policy state changes. Clients
can poll this lightweight endpoint to detect when a full graph refresh
is needed.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster whose epoch to retrieve. Required.

post.summary:
SimulateNetworkGraph previews how the network connectivity graph would
change if the proposed NetworkPolicy modification were applied.
post.description:
Returns the simulated graph and the status of each affected policy.
When include_node_diff is true, also returns the sets of added and removed
connectivity edges.

Modifications to the stackrox or kube-system namespaces are rejected
unless the policy content is identical to the existing policy.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the cluster does not exist.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the cluster to simulate against. Required.

parameters.name: modification
parameters.description:
modification describes the network policies to add, modify, or delete in
the simulation. Must be non-empty and must not affect restricted namespaces
(stackrox, kube-system).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters deployments shown as query-match nodes using StackRox search syntax.

parameters.name: includePorts
parameters.description: If set to true, include port-level information in the network policy graph.

parameters.name: includeNodeDiff
parameters.description:
include_node_diff controls whether added/removed edge diffs between the
current and simulated graphs are computed and returned. When false, only
the simulated graph and policy list are returned.

parameters.name: scope.query
parameters.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

post.summary:
SendNetworkPolicyYAML delivers the proposed network policy modification
YAML to one or more configured notifiers (e.g. Slack, email).
post.description:
The cluster and all specified notifiers must exist. The modification must
be non-empty. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any required field is missing.
Requires Modify access to the Integration resource.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id identifies the target cluster. Required.

parameters.name: modification
parameters.description: modification is the network policy change to send. Must be non-empty.

parameters.name: notifierIds
parameters.description: notifier_ids is the list of notifier IDs to send the YAML to. Required.

get.summary:
GetUndoModificationForDeployment returns the undo record for the most
recent network policy application associated with a specific deployment.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist or has no undo record.

get.summary:
GetUndoModification returns the undo record for the most recent network
policy application to a cluster.
get.description:
The undo record contains the original modification and the modification
needed to revert it.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no undo record exists for the cluster.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster whose undo record to retrieve.

get.summary:
GetDiffFlowsFromUndoModificationForDeployment compares the peers currently
allowed by active NetworkPolicies against those that would be allowed if
the last deployment-level undo modification were applied.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment has no stored undo record.

get.summary: GetNetworkPolicy returns a single NetworkPolicy by ID.
get.description:
The YAML representation of the policy is populated in the response.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no policy with the given ID exists.

get.summary: ExportNodes streams all nodes matching the optional query as a sequence of ExportNodeResponse messages.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description:
timeout is the maximum number of seconds to wait for the export stream to complete.
Zero means no timeout.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters nodes using StackRox search syntax. If empty, all nodes are exported.

get.summary: ListNodes returns all nodes belonging to the specified cluster.
get.description: Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty or does not match a known cluster.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id filters nodes to those belonging to the specified cluster.

get.summary: GetNode returns the node identified by node_id.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description:
cluster_id is included for API compatibility. The node_id uniquely identifies a node
across all clusters and is sufficient to retrieve it.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: node_id is the unique identifier of the node to retrieve.

get.summary: GetNotifiers returns all notifier configurations.
get.description:
Sensitive credential fields are replaced with a placeholder in each
returned notifier. The response is not paginated.

post.summary: PostNotifier creates a new notifier configuration.
post.description:
The id field must be empty; a new ID is assigned and returned. Credentials
must be provided in full. The notifier is validated and its connectivity
is verified before it is persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is set, if required fields are
missing, or if validation or connectivity checks fail.

post.summary:
TestNotifier verifies that the given notifier configuration is reachable
and correctly set up, treating all credential fields as authoritative
(no stored credential reconciliation).
post.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails or if the notifier cannot
be contacted successfully.

post.summary:
TestUpdatedNotifier verifies that the given notifier configuration is
reachable with optional stored credential reconciliation.
post.description:
When update_password is false, credential fields in the request are
replaced with the stored credentials before the test is performed.
When update_password is true, the provided credentials are used directly.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the notifier cannot be contacted or validation
fails. Returns NOT_FOUND if update_password is false and the notifier does
not exist.

get.summary: GetNotifier returns the notifier configuration with the given ID.
get.description:
Sensitive credential fields are replaced with a placeholder in the response.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no notifier with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

delete.summary: DeleteNotifier removes a notifier configuration with the given ID.
delete.description:
Returns FAILED_PRECONDITION if the notifier is still referenced by one
or more policies (check policies before deletion or use force=true).
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the unique identifier of the notifier configuration to delete.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: force, when true, removes the notifier even if it is still referenced by policies.

put.summary:
PutNotifier replaces an existing notifier configuration, treating all
credential fields in the request as authoritative (no stored credential
reconciliation). Prefer UpdateNotifier for password-preserving updates.
put.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails (missing name, type, or UI
endpoint, or invalid endpoint configuration).

patch.summary:
UpdateNotifier modifies an existing notifier configuration with optional
stored credential reconciliation.
patch.description:
When update_password is false, credential fields in the request are
ignored and the stored credentials are preserved. When update_password
is true, credential fields in the request replace stored credentials.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Returns NOT_FOUND if update_password is false and no notifier with the
given ID exists.

get.summary:
Ping confirms Central is reachable and responding. Returns HTTP 200 with
{"status": "ok"} on success. Safe to call without credentials.
ExportPods streams all pods matching the optional query as a sequence of ExportPodResponse messages.
get.description:
Unlike GetPods, ExportPods is not paginated and streams all matching pods. A timeout in
seconds may be specified; if the stream is not completed within that time the request is cancelled.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description:
timeout is the maximum number of seconds to wait for the export stream to complete.
Zero means no timeout.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query filters pods using StackRox search syntax. If empty, all pods are exported.

get.summary: GetPods returns a list of pods matching the optional query filter.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search query syntax to filter by cluster, namespace, deployment, or label.
Results are paginated; at most 1000 pods are returned per request.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: GetPolicyCategories returns the list of policy categories matching the optional query.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search syntax. Returns all categories if the query is empty.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary: PostPolicyCategory creates a new custom policy category.
post.description:
The category name must be between 5 and 128 characters and must not contain newlines or
dollar signs. Returns the created category with its assigned ID.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the request is empty or the name fails validation.

parameters.name: policyCategory
parameters.description:
policyCategory is the category to create. The name must be between 5 and 128 characters
and must not contain newlines or dollar signs.

put.summary: RenamePolicyCategory renames the given policy category.
put.description:
The new name must be between 5 and 128 characters and must not contain newlines or dollar signs.
Returns the updated category.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the new name fails validation.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no category with the given ID exists.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: RenamePolicyCategoryRequest is the request message for RenamePolicyCategory.

get.summary: GetPolicyCategory returns a single policy category by ID.
get.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no category with the given ID exists.

delete.summary: DeletePolicyCategory removes the given policy category permanently.
delete.description:
Default (built-in) categories cannot be deleted. Custom categories used by active policies
are unlinked from those policies on deletion.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no category with the given ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the policy category to delete.

get.summary: ListPolicies returns a paginated, filterable summary list of policies.
get.description:
Accepts a StackRox search query (e.g. "Severity:HIGH+Disabled:false").
Returns up to 1000 policies per request. Results include summary fields
only (id, name, description, severity, disabled, lifecycle_stages, etc.);
use GetPolicy to retrieve the full policy definition including criteria.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary: PostPolicy creates a new policy and activates it immediately.
post.description:
The policy.id field must be empty; Central assigns a UUID on creation and
returns the full policy with the assigned ID. After creation the policy is
added to the active detection engine and synced to all connected Sensors.

Set enable_strict_validation to apply additional validation rules (e.g.
restrictions on environment variable source values) beyond the defaults.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the policy definition is invalid or if id is non-empty.

parameters.name: policy
parameters.description:
policy is the full policy definition to create. The id field must be empty;
Central assigns the ID on creation and returns the populated policy.

parameters.name: enableStrictValidation
parameters.description:
enable_strict_validation enables additional validation rules, such as
restrictions on environment variable source values. Use this when
importing policies from external sources to ensure stricter correctness.

post.summary:
DryRunPolicy evaluates a policy against all current deployments and returns
the list of would-be violations, without saving or enforcing the policy.
post.description:
Only DEPLOY and BUILD lifecycle stages are evaluated; RUNTIME policies are
excluded because runtime detection runs through the process event pipeline,
not the deployment snapshot. Up to 8 deployments are evaluated in parallel.

The returned alerts list each deployment name and the associated violation
messages. No alerts are created, no enforcement actions are taken, and the
policy is not persisted.

For large environments, prefer the async SubmitDryRunPolicyJob / QueryDryRunJobStatus
workflow to avoid request timeouts.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the policy definition fails validation.

post.summary:
SubmitDryRunPolicyJob enqueues an asynchronous dry-run evaluation job and
returns a job ID that can be polled with QueryDryRunJobStatus.
post.description:
Use this for large environments where a synchronous DryRunPolicy call may
time out. The job is scoped to the submitting user; other users cannot
query or cancel it.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the policy definition fails validation.

get.summary: QueryDryRunJobStatus returns the current status of an asynchronous dry-run job.
get.description:
While the job is running, pending=true and result is not set. Once complete,
pending=false and result contains the dry-run output.

Only the user who submitted the job can query its status.

Returns NOT_FOUND (via the job manager) if the job ID is unknown.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: job_id is the opaque identifier returned by SubmitDryRunPolicyJob.

delete.summary: CancelDryRunJob cancels a running asynchronous dry-run job.
delete.description:
Only the user who submitted the job can cancel it.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the job ID is unknown or the job cannot be cancelled.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: job_id is the opaque identifier returned by SubmitDryRunPolicyJob.

post.summary:
ExportPolicies serializes the requested policies for transfer to another
Central instance via ImportPolicies.
post.description:
Internal sort fields (SORT_name, SORT_lifecycleStage, SORT_enforcement) are
stripped from exported policies. All supplied IDs must refer to existing
policies; if any ID is missing the entire request fails.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT with error details (PolicyOperationErrorList) if
any of the requested policy IDs cannot be found.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: ExportPoliciesRequest selects a set of policies to export by ID.

post.summary:
PolicyFromSearch converts a StackRox search query string into a draft policy
definition that can be reviewed and then saved with PostPolicy.
post.description:
Search terms that have no direct policy criteria equivalent (e.g. free-text
fields) are listed in the response's altered_search_terms. Cluster, namespace,
and deployment-label search terms are converted to policy scopes rather than
policy criteria. The resulting policy is not persisted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the search string is empty or cannot be parsed.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: PolicyFromSearchRequest converts a StackRox search query string into a draft policy.

post.summary: ImportPolicies imports a batch of policies exported from another Central instance.
post.description:
Each policy is validated independently. Valid policies are upserted; invalid
ones are reported in the per-policy errors without affecting the rest of the
batch. When metadata.overwrite=true, an existing policy with the same name or
ID is replaced; otherwise a conflict causes that policy's import to fail with
a duplicate_name error.

After a successful import, each imported policy is added to the detection engine
and synced to all connected Sensors. Internal sort fields are stripped from
returned policies.

The response includes one ImportPolicyResponse per submitted policy plus the
all_succeeded summary flag. A partial failure does not return a gRPC error;
inspect each response's succeeded field and errors list instead.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: ImportPoliciesRequest submits one or more policies for import into Central.

post.summary:
ReassessPolicies triggers immediate re-enrichment of all deployments and
re-evaluation of all active policies against the updated data.
post.description:
This invalidates cached image scan metadata and signals the reprocessor
loop to run immediately rather than waiting for the next scheduled cycle.
Use this after updating integrations or policies to force timely re-detection.

get.summary: GetPolicy returns the full policy definition for the given ID.
get.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no policy with that ID exists.

delete.summary: DeletePolicy permanently removes a custom policy by ID.
delete.description:
Default policies (is_default=true) cannot be deleted; use PatchPolicy to
disable them instead. Deleting an already-absent policy is idempotent and
returns success. After deletion the policy is removed from the detection
engine and the change is synced to all connected Sensors.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID is empty or if the policy is a default policy.

put.summary: PutPolicy replaces an existing policy with the supplied definition.
put.description:
The full policy object must be supplied, including all fields that should
be retained. Unlike PatchPolicy, this is not a partial update. After the
update the policy is re-added to the detection engine and synced to Sensors.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the policy definition is invalid.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no policy with the given ID exists.

patch.summary: PatchPolicy applies a partial update to an existing policy.
patch.description:
Currently supports toggling the disabled state only. To enable a policy,
set disabled=false; to disable it, set disabled=true. A disabled policy
remains stored but does not fire alerts or enforce actions.

Internally calls PutPolicy after applying the patch, so the full
policy re-sync with Sensors is triggered.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no policy with the given ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the policy to patch.

get.summary:
GetPolicyMitreVectors returns the MITRE ATT&CK tactic and technique vectors
associated with the policy identified by the given ID.
get.description:
The vectors are expanded from the policy's stored mitre_attack_vectors using
the MITRE ATT&CK knowledge base. Set options.exclude_policy to omit the
full policy object from the response and return only the vectors.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no policy with that ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the policy whose MITRE ATT&CK vectors to retrieve.

parameters.name: options.excludePolicy
parameters.description: If set to true, policy is excluded from the response.

patch.summary: EnableDisablePolicyNotification adds or removes notifiers from a policy.
patch.description:
When disable=false, the listed notifier IDs are added to the policy's
notifier list. Notifiers that are already present are silently skipped.
When disable=true, the listed notifier IDs are removed from the policy's
notifier list. IDs not currently attached are silently skipped.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if policy_id or notifier_ids are empty (for enable).
Returns NOT_FOUND if the policy does not exist.

parameters.name: policyId
parameters.description: policy_id is the UUID of the policy to update.

get.summary:
GetPolicyCategories returns the sorted list of all distinct category names
currently assigned to at least one policy in Central.

post.summary:
GetExistingProbes returns which of the requested probe files are already
cached on Central, allowing the uploader to skip files that are unchanged.

delete.summary: DeleteProcessBaselines deletes baselines matching a StackRox search query.
delete.description:
If confirm is false (dry run), returns num_deleted indicating how many
baselines match without deleting any. If confirm is true, clears the
baseline contents for deployments that still exist and removes baselines
for deployments that have been deleted.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query is empty or malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: query is a StackRox search query selecting the baselines to delete. Required.

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description:
confirm must be set to true to perform actual deletion. If false, the
response indicates how many baselines would be deleted (dry run).

put.summary:
UpdateProcessBaselines adds and/or removes process names from one or more
process baselines.
put.description:
Accepts a list of baseline keys and separate lists of process names to add
and remove. Returns the updated baseline objects and per-key errors for any
failures. After all successful updates, deployment risks are reprocessed
and updated baselines are sent to Sensor.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
UpdateProcessBaselinesRequest adds or removes process names from one or more
process baselines identified by their composite keys.

put.summary:
BulkLockProcessBaselines locks all process baselines in a cluster,
optionally restricted to specific namespaces.
put.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty. Returns success=true if
all baselines were locked without error.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
BulkProcessBaselinesRequest identifies a set of baselines by cluster and
optional namespaces for bulk lock/unlock operations.

put.summary:
BulkUnlockProcessBaselines unlocks all process baselines in a cluster,
optionally restricted to specific namespaces.
put.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if cluster_id is empty. Returns success=true if
all baselines were unlocked without error.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
BulkProcessBaselinesRequest identifies a set of baselines by cluster and
optional namespaces for bulk lock/unlock operations.

get.summary:
GetProcessBaseline returns the process baseline identified by the composite
key (cluster_id, namespace, deployment_id, container_name).
get.description:
If no baseline exists for the key and the deployment is still present, an
empty unlocked baseline is created and returned.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if any key field is missing.
Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist.

parameters.name: key.deploymentId
parameters.description:
The idea is for the keys to be flexible.
Only certain combinations of these will be supported.

put.summary:
LockProcessBaselines locks or unlocks a list of process baselines
identified by their composite keys.
put.description:
When locked is true, the baselines are user-locked; when false, they are
user-unlocked. Returns the updated baseline objects. After updates,
deployment risks are reprocessed and baselines are sent to Sensor.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: LockProcessBaselinesRequest locks or unlocks a list of process baselines.

get.summary: GetListeningEndpoints returns the listening endpoints and the processes that opened them for a given deployment
CountProcesses returns the count of process indicators matching the given query.
get.description:
Accepts StackRox search query syntax (e.g. "Deployment:frontend+Namespace:prod").
Matches all process indicators if the query is empty.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetProcessesByDeployment returns all raw process indicators observed in
the given deployment.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if the deployment does not exist.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_id is empty.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment to retrieve processes for. Required.

get.summary:
GetGroupedProcessByDeployment returns all processes observed in the given
deployment, grouped by executable path and argument string.
get.description:
Each ProcessNameGroup contains the number of distinct containers in which
the process was observed and the individual signal observations per argument
set, sorted by argument string. Results are sorted by executable path.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_id is empty.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment to retrieve processes for. Required.

get.summary:
GetGroupedProcessByDeploymentAndContainer returns all processes observed
in the given deployment grouped by both executable path and container name.
get.description:
Each group is additionally annotated with a suspicious flag that is true
when the process is not in the locked process baseline for that container,
indicating it may be unexpected behavior.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if deployment_id is empty.

parameters.name: deploymentId
parameters.description: deployment_id is the ID of the deployment to retrieve processes for. Required.

get.summary: ListRoleBindings returns all Kubernetes role bindings matching the search query.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search query syntax. An empty query returns all role bindings.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.
Requires read access to the K8sRoleBinding resource.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: GetRoleBinding returns the Kubernetes role binding with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no K8s role binding with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the K8sRoleBinding resource.

get.summary: ListRoles returns all Kubernetes roles matching the search query.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search query syntax. An empty query returns all roles.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.
Requires read access to the K8sRole resource.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: GetRole returns the Kubernetes role with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no K8s role with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the K8sRole resource.

get.summary:
GetSubject returns the Kubernetes subject (user or group) identified by the given name,
together with their cluster-wide and namespace-scoped role bindings.
get.description:
Subjects served from this API are Groups and Users only.
The id in this case is the Name field, since for users and groups that is unique,
and subjects do not have IDs.
Requires read access to the K8sSubject resource.

get.summary:
ListSubjects returns all Kubernetes subjects (users and groups) bound to roles matching
the search query, along with the roles they are bound to.
get.description:
Only role-binding-specific search fields in the query are used to filter bindings.
Additional subject-level filtering is applied on the aggregated results.
Uses StackRox search query syntax. An empty query returns all subjects.
Requires read access to the K8sSubject resource.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: CountReportConfigurations returns the number of report configurations.
get.description:
Applies the same v1-only filter as GetReportConfigurations, so v2 configurations
are excluded from the count. Supports StackRox search query syntax.

Requires View access to WorkflowAdministration.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: GetReportConfigurations returns all v1 report configurations matching the query.
get.description:
Supports StackRox search query syntax to filter results. Only v1 report
configurations are returned; v2 configurations are excluded automatically.
Supports pagination via the query's pagination field.

Requires View access to WorkflowAdministration.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary: PostReportConfiguration creates a report configuration
post.description:
Validates the configuration (notifiers, schedule, resource collection) before
persisting it and registering it with the report scheduler.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the configuration fails validation.
Requires Modify access to WorkflowAdministration and View access to Integration.

get.summary: GetReportConfiguration returns the v1 report configuration with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no configuration with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the ID refers to a v2 report configuration.

Requires View access to WorkflowAdministration.

delete.summary: DeleteReportConfiguration removes a report configuration given its id
delete.description:
Also removes the configuration from the report scheduler. Only v1 configurations
can be deleted via this endpoint.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no configuration with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id is empty or refers to a v2 configuration.
Requires Modify access to WorkflowAdministration.

put.summary: UpdateReportConfiguration updates a report configuration
put.description:
Validates the updated configuration before persisting and re-registering it
with the report scheduler. Only v1 configurations can be updated via this endpoint.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails or the ID refers to a v2 configuration.
Requires Modify access to WorkflowAdministration and View access to Integration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the ID of the report configuration to update.

post.summary: RunReport queues an on-demand execution of the report configuration with the given ID.
post.description:
The report is queued for asynchronous generation and delivered to the configured
email recipients once complete. The call returns immediately after the report is
enqueued; it does not wait for generation to finish.

Only v1 report configurations are accepted. Passing a v2 configuration ID returns
INVALID_ARGUMENT.

Returns NOT_FOUND if no report configuration with the given ID exists.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the configuration belongs to reporting version 2.0.

get.summary: ListCollections returns all collections matching the optional search query.
get.description:
Results are sorted by collection name ascending. Pagination is controlled by the query's
pagination field with a default page size of 1000.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary: CreateCollection creates a new resource collection.
post.description:
The collection name must be non-empty after whitespace trimming. At least one resource selector
or embedded collection ID must be provided. The caller's identity is recorded as the creator.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the name is empty, or if no selectors or embedded collections are provided.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateCollectionRequest is the request message for CreateCollection.

post.summary: DryRunCollection simulates creating or updating a collection without persisting it.
post.description:
Validates the collection definition and, when options.with_matches is true, resolves and
returns the deployments that would match. Set id to simulate an update to an existing
collection; leave id empty to simulate a new collection.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the collection definition is invalid (empty name, no selectors).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: DryRunCollectionRequest is the request message for DryRunCollection.

get.summary: ListCollectionSelectors returns all supported field labels that can be used in resource selectors.
get.description:
Supported selectors include fields such as "Cluster", "Namespace", and "Deployment Label".
Requires read access to the WorkflowAdministration resource.

get.summary: GetCollection returns a single collection by ID.
get.description:
When CollectionDeploymentMatchOptions.with_matches is true, also resolves and returns the
deployments currently matched by the collection's selectors, optionally further filtered
by filter_query.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no collection with the given ID exists.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the collection to retrieve.

parameters.name: options.withMatches
parameters.description: with_matches, when true, resolves the collection's selectors and returns all deployments that match.

parameters.name: options.filterQuery.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

delete.summary: DeleteCollection removes a collection permanently.
delete.description:
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty.
Returns a conflict error if the collection is referenced by another collection or by a
vulnerability report configuration.

patch.summary: UpdateCollection updates an existing collection's name, description, selectors, and embedded collections.
patch.description:
Replaces the full set of selectors and embedded collection references.
The caller's identity is recorded as the last updater.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty, the name is empty, or no selectors or embedded collections are provided.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is the UUID of the collection to update.

get.summary: GetCollectionCount returns the count of collections matching the optional query.
get.description: Matches all collections if the query is empty.

parameters.name: query.query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary:
ComputeEffectiveAccessScope evaluates access scope rules against the current cluster and
namespace inventory and returns the resulting effective access scope.
post.description:
Does not persist anything; not idempotent due to possible changes to clusters
and namespaces. POST is chosen due to potentially large payload.

There are advantages in both keeping the response slim and detailed. If
only IDs of selected clusters and namespaces are included, response
latency and processing time are lower but the caller shall overlay the
response with its view of the world which is susceptible to consistency
issues. Listing all clusters and namespaces with related metadata is
convenient for the caller but bloat the message with secondary data.

We let the caller decide what level of detail they would like to have:

  - Minimal, when only roots of included subtrees are listed by their
    IDs. Clusters can be either INCLUDED (its namespaces are included but
    are not listed) or PARTIAL (at least one namespace is explicitly
    included). Namespaces can only be INCLUDED.

  - Standard [default], when all known clusters and namespaces are listed
    with their IDs and names. Clusters can be INCLUDED (all its
    namespaces are explicitly listed as INCLUDED), PARTIAL (all its
    namespaces are explicitly listed, some as INCLUDED and some as
    EXCLUDED), and EXCLUDED (all its namespaces are explicitly listed as
    EXCLUDED). Namespaces can be either INCLUDED or EXCLUDED.

  - High, when every cluster and namespace is augmented with metadata.

Requires read access to the Access, Cluster, and Namespace resources.

parameters.name: accessScope
parameters.description: access_scope holds the rules to evaluate against the current cluster/namespace inventory.

parameters.name: detail
parameters.description:
detail controls how much information is returned per cluster/namespace. Defaults to STANDARD.

 - STANDARD: STANDARD lists all known clusters and namespaces with INCLUDED/PARTIAL/EXCLUDED state and their names.
 - MINIMAL: MINIMAL lists only the roots of included subtrees by ID; useful for low-latency checks.
 - HIGH: HIGH augments every cluster and namespace with additional metadata.

get.summary: GetMyPermissions returns the aggregated permissions of the authenticated caller.
get.description:
Permissions are aggregated across all roles assigned to the caller. For each resource,
the highest access level across all roles is returned.
Requires any authenticated session (no specific resource permission needed).

get.summary: ListPermissionSets returns all non-ephemeral permission sets, sorted alphabetically by name.
get.description: Requires read access to the Access resource.

post.summary: PostPermissionSet creates a new permission set.
post.description:
PermissionSet.id is disallowed in request and set in response.
Ephemeral permission sets cannot be created via this API.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is set or validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: This encodes a set of permissions for StackRox resources.

get.summary: GetPermissionSet returns the permission set with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no permission set with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

delete.summary: DeletePermissionSet removes the permission set with the given ID.
delete.description: Requires write access to the Access resource.

put.summary: PutPermissionSet replaces an existing permission set.
put.description:
Ephemeral permission sets cannot be modified via this API.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: id is generated and cannot be changed.

get.summary: GetResources returns the list of all ACS resource names that can be referenced in permission sets.
get.description: Requires any authenticated session.

get.summary: GetRoles returns all non-ephemeral roles, sorted alphabetically by name.
get.description: Requires read access to the Access resource.

get.summary: GetRole returns the role with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no role with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

delete.summary: DeleteRole removes the role with the given ID.
delete.description: Requires write access to the Access resource.

post.summary: CreateRole creates a new role with the given name.
post.description:
The name in the URL path must match role.name if role.name is provided.
Ephemeral roles cannot be created via this API.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the name is mismatched or the role already exists.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: name is the role name taken from the URL path; it must match role.name if role.name is set.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: role is the role definition to create.

put.summary: UpdateRole replaces an existing role definition.
put.description:
Ephemeral roles cannot be modified via this API.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails or the role does not exist.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: `name` and `description` are provided by the user and can be changed.

get.summary:
GetClustersForPermissions returns the cluster ID/name pairs accessible to the caller
for the specified permissions.
get.description:
Returns the list of cluster ID and cluster name pairs that have at least read allowed
by the scope of the requesting user for the list of requested permissions.
Effective access scopes are only considered for input permissions that have
cluster scope or narrower (i.e. global permissions from the input are ignored).

If the input only contains permissions at global level, the output will be an empty list.

If no permission is given in input, all clusters allowed by the requester scope for
any permission with cluster scope or narrower will be part of the response.
Requires any authenticated session.

parameters.name: permissions
parameters.description:
permissions is the list of ACS resource names to check. Only permissions with cluster scope
or narrower are considered; global-scoped permissions are ignored.
If empty, all cluster-scoped permissions are considered.

get.summary:
GetNamespacesForClusterAndPermissions returns the namespace ID/name pairs within a cluster
that are accessible to the caller for the specified permissions.
get.description:
Returns the list of namespace ID and namespace name pairs that belong to the requested
cluster and for which the user has at least read access granted for the list of
requested permissions that have namespace scope or narrower (i.e. global and cluster
permissions from the input are ignored).

If the input only contains permissions at global or cluster level, the output will be
an empty list.

If no permission is given in input, all namespaces allowed by the requester scope for
any permission with namespace scope or narrower will be part of the response.
Requires any authenticated session.

parameters.name: clusterId
parameters.description: cluster_id is the ID of the cluster whose namespaces to filter.

parameters.name: permissions
parameters.description:
permissions is the list of ACS resource names to check. Only permissions with namespace scope
or narrower are considered; global and cluster-scoped permissions are ignored.
If empty, all namespace-scoped permissions are considered.

get.summary: ListSimpleAccessScopes returns all non-ephemeral simple access scopes, sorted alphabetically by name.
get.description: Requires read access to the Access resource.

post.summary: PostSimpleAccessScope creates a new simple access scope.
post.description:
SimpleAccessScope.id is disallowed in request and set in response.
Ephemeral access scopes cannot be created via this API.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is set or validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Simple access scope is a (simple) selection criteria for scoped resources.
It does *not* allow multi-component AND-rules nor set operations on names.

get.summary: GetSimpleAccessScope returns the simple access scope with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no access scope with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

delete.summary: DeleteSimpleAccessScope removes the simple access scope with the given ID.
delete.description: Requires write access to the Access resource.

put.summary: PutSimpleAccessScope replaces an existing simple access scope.
put.description:
Ephemeral access scopes cannot be modified via this API.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if validation fails.
Requires write access to the Access resource.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: `id` is generated and cannot be changed.

get.summary:
Search performs a global search across one or more resource categories
and returns matched resources sorted by relevance score.
get.description:
Accepts the StackRox query syntax (e.g. "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production").
When categories is empty, all supported searchable categories are queried
(ALERTS, DEPLOYMENTS, IMAGES, POLICIES, SECRETS, NAMESPACES, NODES, CLUSTERS,
SERVICE_ACCOUNTS, ROLES, ROLEBINDINGS, SUBJECTS, IMAGE_INTEGRATIONS, POLICY_CATEGORIES).

For ALERTS, results are only returned when the query explicitly filters on
an alert field (e.g. "Severity", "Policy"); otherwise the category is skipped
and its count is reported as 0.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query string cannot be parsed or if an
unsupported category is requested.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Use the Options RPC to discover
valid field names for each category. Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Namespace:stackrox"

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description:
categories restricts the search to specific resource types. When empty,
the service searches across all supported categories.

 - SEARCH_UNSET: SEARCH_UNSET means no category was specified. When passed to Search or
Autocomplete it causes the service to search across all supported categories.

get.summary: ListSecrets returns lightweight summaries of secrets matching the optional query.
get.description:
Uses StackRox search query syntax to filter by cluster, namespace, or secret name.
Results are paginated; at most 1000 secrets are returned per request.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary: GetSecret returns the secret with the given ID, including its deployment relationships.
get.description:
The response includes all deployments in the same cluster and namespace that reference this
secret by name, resolved at query time.
Returns NOT_FOUND if no secret with the given ID exists.

get.summary: CountSecrets returns the total number of secrets matching the optional query.
get.description:
Supports StackRox search query syntax to filter by cluster, namespace, or secret name.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query syntax is malformed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

post.summary: TriggerSensorUpgrade initiates an upgrade for the sensor running in the specified cluster.

get.summary:
GetSensorUpgradeConfig returns the current sensor auto-upgrade configuration,
including whether auto-upgrade is enabled and whether the feature is supported.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if no configuration has been persisted yet.

post.summary: UpdateSensorUpgradeConfig persists a new sensor auto-upgrade configuration.
post.description:
When enable_auto_upgrade is set to true, Central will automatically trigger upgrades for
sensors that report a version mismatch. Setting it to true fails with INVALID_ARGUMENT
if the auto-upgrade feature is not supported in this deployment.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: UpdateSensorUpgradeConfigRequest is the request to update the sensor auto-upgrade configuration.

post.summary: TriggerSensorCertRotation initiates a certificate rotation for the sensor in the specified cluster.
post.description:
This renews the sensor's mTLS certificates without performing a full sensor upgrade or redeploy.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the cluster ID is empty.

get.summary:
ListServiceAccounts returns service accounts matching the search query,
with their associated roles and deployment relationships.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query is a StackRox search query string. Multiple field:value pairs are
joined with "+" (AND). Multiple values for one field are joined with ","
(OR). Example: "Severity:CRITICAL+Cluster:production"

get.summary:
GetServiceAccount returns a single service account by ID, including its
bound K8s roles, cluster roles, and deployment relationships.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if the service account ID does not exist.

get.summary: GetAuthorities returns the CA certificates currently trusted by Central.
get.description:
These are the root certificates that can be used to verify service identity certificates
issued by Central.

get.summary: GetServiceIdentities returns metadata for all service identities registered in Central.
get.description: Returns the list of known identities (without private keys, which are never stored).

post.summary: CreateServiceIdentity issues a new mTLS certificate and private key for a service.
post.description:
The generated key pair is returned in the response and is never stored by Central.
If the credentials are lost, a new identity must be created; the existing one cannot be recovered.
Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if id is empty or type is UNKNOWN_SERVICE.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: CreateServiceIdentityRequest is the request to create a new mTLS service identity.

get.summary:
ListSignatureIntegrations returns all configured signature integrations,
sorted alphabetically by name.

post.summary: PostSignatureIntegration creates a new signature integration.
post.description:
The id field must not be set; a new ID is assigned and returned. If this
is the first signature integration added, Central immediately triggers a
full reprocessing of signature verifications for all images.

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the id field is set or if validation fails.

get.summary: GetSignatureIntegration returns the signature integration with the given ID.
get.description: Returns NOT_FOUND if no integration with the given ID exists.

delete.summary:
DeleteSignatureIntegration removes the signature integration with the
given ID and triggers reprocessing of signature verification results for
all images.

put.summary: PutSignatureIntegration replaces an existing signature integration.
put.description:
If the verification configuration (e.g. public keys) has changed, Central
triggers reprocessing of signature verification results for all images.
If the configuration is unchanged, no reprocessing is triggered.

get.summary:
GetConfig returns the current phone-home telemetry client configuration
for the authenticated user.
get.description:
The response includes a hashed user ID derived from the caller's auth
identity, the telemetry endpoint URL, and a storage key (empty when
the telemetry client is disabled).

post.summary:
PostConfigReload triggers Central to re-read its telemetry configuration
from the environment and reconfigure the phone-home client accordingly.

get.summary:
GetTelemetryConfiguration is deprecated and returns enabled=false
unconditionally. It was previously used to check whether periodic
telemetry collection was enabled.
get.description: Deprecated: use GetConfig instead.

put.summary:
ConfigureTelemetry is deprecated and has no effect. It was previously
used to enable or disable periodic telemetry collection.
put.description: Deprecated: use PostConfigReload instead.

get.summary: GetUsers returns all users who have previously authenticated to Central.
get.description: Requires read access to the Access resource.

get.summary: GetUser returns the user with the given ID.
get.description:
Returns NOT_FOUND if no user with the specified ID exists.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

get.summary:
GetUsersAttributes returns the deduplicated set of identity-provider attribute tuples
across all known users.
get.description:
Each tuple contains the auth provider ID, attribute key, and attribute value. Duplicates
across users are collapsed. This endpoint is used to populate group mapping configuration UIs.
Requires read access to the Access resource.

get.summary:
VulnMgmtExportWorkloads streams vulnerability data for all deployments
matching the request filter.
get.description:
Each stream message contains one deployment, its deduplicated container
images with full CVE scan data, and the number of live pods. The entire
export runs within a single read transaction, so all messages represent a
consistent snapshot of the database at the time the call was made.

Images are cached in an LRU cache (capacity 1000) during a single call so
that images shared across multiple deployments are fetched from the
database only once.

The HTTP response body is a newline-delimited sequence of JSON objects:
  {"result": {"deployment": {...}, "images": [...], "livePods": N}}
  {"result": {"deployment": {...}, "images": [...], "livePods": N}}

Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT if the query string cannot be parsed.
Returns PERMISSION_DENIED if the caller lacks read access to Deployment
or Image resources.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description:
timeout is the maximum duration in seconds to wait for the export to
complete. When set, the server cancels the streaming operation after the
given number of seconds. When 0 or unset, no timeout is applied beyond
the underlying connection deadline.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
query constrains which deployments are included in the export using
StackRox search syntax. Pairs of `Search Option:Value` are joined by `+`.
When empty, all deployments visible to the caller are exported.

Example (URL-encoded):
  curl "$ROX_ENDPOINT/v1/export/vuln-mgmt/workloads?query=Deployment%3Ascanner%2BNamespace%3Astackrox"

Supported search fields include Deployment, Namespace, Cluster, Label,
and any other field accepted by the deployment search index.

See https://docs.openshift.com/acs/operating/search-filter.html for
the full query syntax reference.

type.description:
Resource type associated with the event. An event may refer to an underlying resource
such as a particular image. In that case, the resource type will be filled here.

id.description:
Resource ID associated with the event. If an event refers to an underlying resource,
the resource ID identifies the underlying resource. The resource ID is not guaranteed
to be set, depending on the context of the administration event.

name.description:
Resource name associated with the event. If an event refers to an underlying resource,
the resource name identifies the underlying resource. The resource name is not guaranteed
to be set, depending on the context of the administration event.

AdministrationEventResource.description: Resource holds all information about the resource associated with the event.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

id.description: UUID of the event.

level.description:
Level associated with the event. The level is categorized into danger, warn, info,
success.

message.description:
Message associated with the event. The message may include detailed information
for this particular event.

hint.description:
Hint associated with the event. The hint may include different information based
on the type of event. It can include instructions to resolve an event, or
informational hints.

domain.description:
Domain associated with the event. An event's domain outlines the feature domain where
the event was created from. As an example, this might be "Image Scanning".
In case of events that cannot be tied to a specific domain, this will be "General".

numOccurrences.description:
Occurrences associated with the event. When events may occur multiple times, the
occurrences track the amount.

lastOccurredAt.description: Specifies the time when the event has last occurred.

createdAt.description: Specifies the time when the event has been created.

v1AdministrationEvent.description:
AdministrationEvents are administrative events emitted by Central. They are used to create
transparency for users for asynchronous, background tasks. Events are part of Central's
system health view.

v1AdministrationEventLevel.description: AdministrationEventLevel exposes the different levels of events.

v1AdministrationEventType.description: AdministrationEventType exposes the different types of events.

from.description: Matches events with last_occurred_at after a specific timestamp, i.e. the lower boundary.

until.description: Matches events with last_occurred_at before a specific timestamp, i.e. the upper boundary.

domain.description: Matches events from a specific domain.

resourceType.description: Matches events associated with a specific resource type.

type.description: Matches events based on their type.

level.description: Matches events based on their level.

count.description: The total number of events after filtering and deduplication.

sortOptions.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

aggregateBy.description: This field is under development. It is not supported on any REST APIs.

maxNodesAt.description: max_nodes_at is the timestamp (with ~1h accuracy) when the peak node count was observed.

maxNodes.description: max_nodes is the highest node count observed within the requested time range.

maxCpuUnitsAt.description: max_cpu_units_at is the timestamp (with ~1h accuracy) when the peak CPU unit count was observed.

maxCpuUnits.description: max_cpu_units is the highest CPU unit count observed within the requested time range.

v1MaxSecuredUnitsUsageResponse.description:
MaxSecuredUnitsUsageResponse holds the maximum values of the secured nodes
and CPU Units (as reported by Kubernetes) with the time at which these
values were aggregated, with the aggregation period accuracy (1h).

numNodes.description: num_nodes is the total number of nodes currently reported across all connected clusters.

numCpuUnits.description:
num_cpu_units is the total number of CPU units (logical cores) currently reported
across all connected clusters.

v1SecuredUnitsUsageResponse.description:
SecuredUnitsUsageResponse holds the values of the currently observable
administration usage metrics.

name.description: Name of the policy.  Must be unique.

severity.description: Defines how severe a violation from this policy is.  Possible values are UNSET_SEVERITY, LOW_SEVERITY, MEDIUM_SEVERITY, HIGH_SEVERITY, and CRITICAL_SEVERITY.

enforcementActions.description:
FAIL_DEPLOYMENT_CREATE_ENFORCEMENT takes effect only if admission control webhook is configured to enforce on object creates/updates.
FAIL_KUBE_REQUEST_ENFORCEMENT takes effect only if admission control webhook is enabled to listen on exec and port-forward events.

  a client ID and no client secret.

- "disable_offline_access_scope": set to "true" if no offline tokens shall be issued.

claimMappings.description:
Specifies claims from IdP token that will be copied to Rox token attributes.

Each key in this map contains a path in IdP token we want to map. Path is separated by "." symbol.
For example, if IdP token payload looks like:


{

     "a": {

         "b" : "c",

         "d": true,

         "e": [ "val1", "val2", "val3" ],

         "f": [ true, false, false ],

         "g": 123.0,

         "h": [ 1, 2, 3]

     }

}


then "a.b" would be a valid key and "a.z" is not.

We support the following types of claims:
* string(path "a.b")
* bool(path "a.d")
* string array(path "a.e")
* bool array (path "a.f.")

We do NOT support the following types of claims:
* complex claims(path "a")
* float/integer claims(path "a.g")
* float/integer array claims(path "a.h")

Each value in this map contains a Rox token attribute name we want to add claim to.
If, for example, value is "groups", claim would be found in "external_user.Attributes.groups" in token.

Note: we only support this feature for OIDC auth provider.

storageTraitsMutabilityMode.description:
EXPERIMENTAL.
NOTE: Please refer from using MutabilityMode for the time being. It will be replaced in the future (ROX-14276).
MutabilityMode specifies whether and how an object can be modified. Default
is ALLOW_MUTATE and means there are no modification restrictions; this is equivalent
to the absence of MutabilityMode specification. ALLOW_MUTATE_FORCED forbids all
modifying operations except object removal with force bit on.

Be careful when changing the state of this field. For example, modifying an
object from ALLOW_MUTATE to ALLOW_MUTATE_FORCED is allowed but will prohibit any further
changes to it, including modifying it back to ALLOW_MUTATE.

storageTraitsOrigin.description:
Origin specifies the origin of an object.
Objects can have five different origins:
- IMPERATIVE: the object was created via the API. This is assumed by default.
- DEFAULT: the object is a default object, such as default roles, access scopes etc.
- DECLARATIVE: the object is created via declarative configuration.
- DECLARATIVE_ORPHANED: the object is created via declarative configuration and then unsuccessfully deleted(for example, because it is referenced by another object)
- EPHEMERAL: the object is created via an internal API, generated on the fly and meant to be ephemeral.
Based on the origin, different rules apply to the objects.
Objects with the DECLARATIVE origin are not allowed to be modified via API, only via declarative configuration.
Additionally, they may not reference objects with the IMPERATIVE or EPHEMERAL origin.
Objects with the DEFAULT origin are not allowed to be modified via either API or declarative configuration.
They may be referenced by all other objects.
Objects with the IMPERATIVE origin are allowed to be modified via API, not via declarative configuration.
They may reference all other objects.
Objects with the EPHEMERAL origin are neither allowed to be modified via API, nor via declarative configuration.
They may reference all other objects.
Objects with the DECLARATIVE_ORPHANED origin are not allowed to be modified via either API or declarative configuration.
DECLARATIVE_ORPHANED resource can become DECLARATIVE again if it is redefined in declarative configuration.
Objects with this origin will be cleaned up from the system immediately after they are not referenced by other resources anymore.
They may be referenced by all other objects.

ClusterUpgradeStatusUpgradability.description:
- SENSOR_VERSION_HIGHER: SENSOR_VERSION_HIGHER occurs when we detect that the sensor
is running a newer version than this Central. This is unexpected,
but can occur depending on the patches a customer does.
In this case, we will NOT automatically "upgrade" the sensor,
since that would be a downgrade, even if the autoupgrade setting is
on. The user will be allowed to manually trigger the upgrade, but they are
strongly discouraged from doing so without upgrading Central first, since this
is an unsupported configuration.

AdmissionControlHealthInfo carries data about admission control deployment but does not include admission control health status
derived from this data.
Aggregated admission control health status is not included because it is derived in central and not in the component that
first reports AdmissionControlHealthInfo (sensor).

The following fields are made optional/nullable because there can be errors when trying to obtain them and
 the default value of 0 might be confusing with the actual value 0. In case an error happens when trying to obtain
 a certain field, it will be absent (instead of having the default value).

lastAuditId.title: Previously received audit id. May be empty

For internal use only

helmConfig.description: The Helm configuration of a cluster is only present in case the cluster is Helm- or Operator-managed.

scannerHealthInfo.description:
scanner_health_info is filled when the scanner is deployed on a secured cluster (so called "local scanner").
Please do not confuse this with the default scanner deployment on a central cluster.

sensorHealthStatus.description: The following _health_status fields provide aggregated health status of the respective components and are assigned by central.

lastContact.description:
For sensors not having health capability, this will be filled with gRPC connection poll. Otherwise,
this timestamp will be updated by central pipeline when message is processed.

Note: we use this setting to guard against a specific attack vector during CRS-based cluster registration.
Assuming that a CRS was used to register a cluster A and the CRS is leaked, an attacker shall not be able
to re-run the CRS-flow which would then equip the attacker with a certificate & key issued to the cluster A.
As countermeasure we only allow re-running the CRS-flow only as long as the last_contact field is empty,
indicating that the legit cluster A's sensor has not yet connected with the CRS-issued service certificates.

healthInfoComplete.title: To track cases such as when sensor is healthy, but collector status data is unavailable because the sensor is on an old version

name.description:
Name represents the name under which the cluster is registered with the
cloud provider. In case of self managed OpenShift it is the name chosen
by the OpenShift installer.

id.description:
Id represents a unique ID under which the cluster is registered with the
cloud provider. Not all cluster types have an id. For all OpenShift
clusters, this is the Red Hat `cluster_id` registered with OCM.

storageClusterMetadata.description: ClusterMetadata contains metadata information about the cluster infrastructure.

DEPRECATEDLastContact.description: This field has been deprecated starting release 49.0. Use healthStatus.lastContact instead.

The progress of the current or most recent upgrade, if any,
Note that we don't store any historical data -- the moment
a new upgrade attempt is triggered, we overwrite
information from the previous attempt.
