storageCollectorHealthInfo.description:
CollectorHealthInfo carries data about collector deployment but does not include collector health status derived from this data.
Aggregated collector health status is not included because it is derived in central and not in the component that
first reports CollectorHealthInfo (sensor).

storageCompleteClusterConfig.description:
Encodes a complete cluster configuration minus ID/Name identifiers
including static and dynamic settings.

storageDynamicClusterConfig.description: The difference between Static and Dynamic cluster config is that Dynamic values are sent over the Central to Sensor gRPC connection. This has the benefit of allowing for "hot reloading" of values without restarting Secured cluster components.

statusErrors.description: Collection of errors that occurred while trying to obtain scanner health info.

storageScannerHealthInfo.description:
ScannerHealthInfo represents health info of a scanner instance that is deployed on a secured cluster (so called "local scanner").
When the scanner is deployed on a central cluster, the following message is NOT used.
ScannerHealthInfo carries data about scanner deployment but does not include scanner health status
derived from this data.
Aggregated scanner health status is not included because it is derived in central and not in the component that
first reports ScannerHealthInfo (sensor).

The following fields are made optional/nullable because there can be errors when trying to obtain them and
 the default value of 0 might be confusing with the actual value 0. In case an error happens when trying to obtain
 a certain field, it will be absent (instead of having the default value).

storageSensorDeploymentIdentification.description:
StackRoxDeploymentIdentification aims at uniquely identifying a StackRox Sensor deployment. It is used to determine
whether a sensor connection comes from a sensor pod that has restarted or was recreated (possibly after a network
partition), or from a deployment in a different namespace or cluster.

storageStaticClusterConfig.description: The difference between Static and Dynamic cluster config is that Static values are not sent over the Central to Sensor gRPC connection. They are used, for example, to generate manifests that can be used to set up the Secured Cluster's k8s components. They are *not* dynamically reloaded.

mainImageRepository.description:
main_image_repository is the default container image repository for the main StackRox component,
derived from the Central deployment's image flavor environment variable.

collectorImageRepository.description:
collector_image_repository is the default container image repository for the Collector DaemonSet,
derived from the Central deployment's image flavor environment variable.

kernelSupportAvailable.description:
kernel_support_available indicates whether eBPF/kernel probe sources are reachable from Central,
which determines the runtime collection methods available to Secured Clusters.

v1ClusterDefaultsResponse.description: ClusterDefaultsResponse contains environment-level defaults used when registering new Secured Clusters.

clusterRetentionInfo.description:
cluster_retention_info describes automatic-deletion scheduling for this cluster.
Only present when sensor health is UNHEALTHY and a retention policy is configured.

v1ClusterResponse.description:
ClusterResponse wraps a single cluster with optional decommissioning retention information.
The retention info is only populated when the cluster's sensor health is UNHEALTHY and
the system decommissioned-cluster retention policy is enabled.

clusterIdToRetentionInfo.description:
cluster_id_to_retention_info maps UNHEALTHY cluster IDs to their decommission retention info.
Only clusters whose sensor health status is UNHEALTHY and for which a retention policy is
configured will appear in this map.

v1ClustersList.description: ClustersList is the response for listing multiple Secured Clusters.

isExcluded.description:
is_excluded indicates whether a cluster is protected from automatic deletion
due to a matching label in the decommissioned cluster retention configuration.

daysUntilDeletion.description:
days_until_deletion is the number of days remaining before Central automatically
deletes this cluster if its sensor health remains UNHEALTHY. Only present when
retention is configured and the cluster is not excluded.

v1KernelSupportAvailableResponse.description: KernelSupportAvailableResponse is deprecated. Use ClusterDefaultsResponse instead.

clusterId.description: cluster_id identifies the cluster this run was executed against.

standardId.description: standard_id identifies the compliance standard used for this run.

startTime.description: start_time is when the compliance run began execution.

finishTime.description: finish_time is when the compliance run completed; zero if still in progress.

state.description: state is the current lifecycle stage of this run.

errorMessage.description: error_message contains details when the run failed; empty on success.

v1ComplianceRun.description: ComplianceRun represents a single compliance scan execution for a cluster/standard pair.

clusterId.description: cluster_id is the ID of the cluster to target. Use "*" to target all clusters.

standardId.description: standard_id is the ID of the compliance standard to target. Use "*" to target all standards.

v1ComplianceRunSelection.description:
ComplianceRunSelection specifies a cluster/standard pair for compliance run targeting.
Use "*" as a wildcard to target all clusters or all standards.

v1ComplianceRunState.description:
State describes the lifecycle stage of a compliance run.

 - READY: READY indicates the run is scheduled and waiting to start.
 - STARTED: STARTED indicates the run has been dispatched to the sensor.
 - WAIT_FOR_DATA: WAIT_FOR_DATA indicates the run is waiting for compliance data from the cluster.
 - EVALUTING_CHECKS: EVALUTING_CHECKS indicates the run is actively evaluating compliance checks.
 - FINISHED: FINISHED indicates the run has completed (successfully or with errors).

invalidRunIds.description: invalid_run_ids contains IDs from the request that were not found.

selection.description: selection specifies which clusters and standards to run. Use "*" in either field for all.

implemented.description:
implemented indicates this control has an automated check in StackRox.
Controls where implemented=false are informational only and will not
appear in scan results.

interpretationText.description:
interpretation_text explains how StackRox evaluates this control and what
evidence is collected. Only present when implemented=true.

v1ComplianceControl.description:
ComplianceControl represents a single requirement or check within a
compliance standard (e.g. CIS Kubernetes 1.1.1 or NIST 800-53 AC-2).

numImplementedChecks.description:
num_implemented_checks is the count of controls in this group that have
automated checks implemented in this StackRox version.

v1ComplianceControlGroup.description:
ComplianceControlGroup is a logical grouping of related controls within a
compliance standard (e.g. a section or chapter).

v1ComplianceStandard.description:
ComplianceStandard is the full definition of a compliance standard,
including its metadata, control groups, and individual controls.

numImplementedChecks.description:
num_implemented_checks is the count of controls that have automated checks
implemented in this StackRox version.

this standard evaluates. Controls within this standard may target any
subset of these scopes.

operator (e.g. OpenShift Compliance Operator) rather than built into
StackRox. Dynamic standards may not have full scan result support.

hideScanResults.description:
hideScanResults controls whether scan results for this standard are
suppressed in aggregated views. Set via UpdateComplianceStandardConfig.

v1ComplianceStandardMetadata.description:
ComplianceStandardMetadata describes a compliance standard registered in
StackRox, such as CIS Kubernetes, NIST 800-53, NIST 800-190, PCI DSS 3.2,
or HIPAA 164.

v1ComplianceStandardMetadataScope.description:
Scope enumerates the Kubernetes entity types that a compliance standard
evaluates. A standard may target multiple scopes simultaneously.

 - CLUSTER: CLUSTER indicates the standard evaluates cluster-level configuration.
 - NAMESPACE: NAMESPACE indicates the standard evaluates namespace-level resources.
 - DEPLOYMENT: DEPLOYMENT indicates the standard evaluates workload deployments.
 - NODE: NODE indicates the standard evaluates individual cluster nodes.

results.description:
results contains the most recent successful run's per-entity check results,
broken down by cluster, node, deployment, and machine config.

failedRuns.description:
failed_runs lists metadata for any runs that failed before or after the
most recent successful run. Useful for diagnosing scan errors.

v1GetComplianceRunResultsResponse.description:
GetComplianceRunResultsResponse contains the results of a compliance scan
run for a specific cluster and standard.

standard.description:
standard is the full compliance standard definition including all control
groups and individual controls.

standards.description:
standards is the list of active compliance standards. Only standards that
are active for at least one secured cluster are included.

labels.description:
A list of labels to aggregate on. The complete list of labels for the
given metric group can be found in the documentation, or in the API
error message, returned when an invalid label is attempted to be added.

includeFilters.description:
A map of label name to a filter regular expression for this label
value. See the RE2 syntax reference:
https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax. If include_filters are
specified, a metric record is only counted if all label values match
the according label expression. Patterns are full-match only
(automatically wrapped with ^ and $).

excludeFilters.description:
A map of label name to a filter regular expression for this label
value. See the RE2 syntax reference:
https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax. If exclude_filters are
specified, a metric record is dropped if any label value matches the
according label expression. Patterns are full-match only (automatically
wrapped with ^ and $). Exclude filters are applied after include
filters.

gatheringPeriodMinutes.description:
The gathering period for periodically gathered metrics. If set to zero,
gathering is disabled.

descriptors.description:
Metric descriptors is a map of metric names to the list of allowed
labels.

PrometheusMetricsGroup.description:
A group is a collection of metrics that are computed by the same
aggregator. Metrics in a group may use different subsets of a complete list
of labels supported by the aggregator.

deletedRuntimeRetentionDurationDays.description: This runtime alert retention configuration takes precedence after `allRuntimeRetentionDurationDays`.

allRuntimeRetentionDurationDays.description:
This runtime alert retention configuration has highest precedence. All runtime alerts, including attempted
alerts and deleted deployment alerts, are deleted even if respective retention is longer.

attemptedRuntimeRetentionDurationDays.description: This runtime alert retention configuration has lowest precedence.

metrics.description:
This field defines groups of custom Prometheus metrics to be exposed by the
backend on the API endpoint.

numDays.description: num_days is the duration in days for this expiry option. Must be >= 1 when enabled is true.

enabled.description: enabled controls whether this duration is offered as an expiry choice in the UI.

v1DayOption.description:
DayOption defines a selectable number-of-days duration that administrators can enable
as a pre-set expiry option for vulnerability exception requests.

regex.description:
regex is the built-in default RE2 pattern used to match namespaces that belong to
Red Hat layered products (e.g. OpenShift operators).

config is the complete Central configuration, including both public and private sub-configs.
Both public_config and private_config must be set; omitting either returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.

rules is the ordered list of namespace-matching rules. Each rule must have a non-empty
name and a valid RE2 namespace regex. Returns INVALID_ARGUMENT for empty names, empty
regexes, or malformed regex patterns.

PutPlatformComponentConfigRequest carries the namespace-matching rules used to classify
workloads as platform components rather than user workloads.

config.description:
config is the new vulnerability exception configuration to apply.
Must be non-nil and pass all validation rules (see VulnerabilityExceptionConfig).

v1UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionConfigRequ.description:
UpdateVulnerabilityExceptionConfigRequest carries the desired vulnerability exception
configuration to store. All expiry_options validation rules apply.

config.description: config is the vulnerability exception configuration as stored after the update.

expiryOptions.description: expiry_options defines the expiry modes available when creating vulnerability exceptions.

v1VulnerabilityExceptionConfig.description:
VulnerabilityExceptionConfig controls which expiry options are available when users
create vulnerability deferral or false-positive exception requests.

dayOptions.description:
day_options lists the pre-set day-count durations offered as expiry choices.
At least one DayOption must be provided; at least one must be enabled.
All enabled entries must have a unique num_days value >= 1.

fixableCveOptions.description:
fixable_cve_options configures expiry conditions tied to CVE fixability status.
Must be present; at least one of all_fixable or any_fixable should be true.

customDate.description:
custom_date, when true, allows the UI to display a date picker so users can
specify an arbitrary expiry date for the exception.

indefinite.description: indefinite, when true, allows users to create exceptions with no expiry date.

v1VulnerabilityExceptionConfigExpiryOpti.description:
ExpiryOptions defines the full set of expiry modes presented to users when creating
a vulnerability exception. At least one option must be enabled.

allFixable.description:
all_fixable, when true, expires the deferral request only when **all** vulnerabilities
in the request become fixable.

anyFixable.description:
any_fixable, when true, expires the deferral request as soon as **any** vulnerability
in the request becomes fixable.

v1VulnerabilityExceptionConfigFixableCVE.description: FixableCVEOptions defines expiry conditions based on whether vulnerabilities become fixable.

cves.description:
cves is the list of NVD vulnerability identifiers to suppress.
These are the `cve` field values of `storage.CVE` (e.g. "CVE-2021-44832"),
and *not* the internal `id` field.

duration.description:
duration specifies how long the CVE should remain suppressed (snoozed).
After this duration elapses, the CVE returns to the OBSERVED state automatically.
In JSON format, encode as a string with a trailing "s" suffix representing seconds;
nanoseconds are expressed as fractional seconds (e.g. "3s", "3.000000001s", "3.000001s").

v1SuppressCVERequest.description: SuppressCVERequest is the request message for suppressing (snoozeing) one or more CVEs.

cves.description:
cves is the list of NVD vulnerability identifiers to unsuppress.
These are the `cve` field values of `storage.CVE` (e.g. "CVE-2021-44832"),
and *not* the internal `id` field.

v1UnsuppressCVERequest.description: UnsuppressCVERequest is the request message for lifting suppression from one or more CVEs.

DBExportManifestEncodingType.description: The encoding of the file data in the restore body, usually for compression purposes.

pos.description: pos is the byte position in the restore data stream at which to resume.

DBRestoreProcessStatusResumeInfo.description:
ResumeInfo contains the byte offset from which a paused restore can be
resumed.

path.description: The full path of the file.

bytesSize.description: The size of the file, in bytes. 0 if unknown.

DBRestoreRequestHeaderLocalFileInfo.description:
LocalFileInfo provides information about the file on the local machine of the user initiating the restore
process, in order to provide information to other users about ongoing restore processes.

formatName.description: format_name is the human-readable identifier for this export format.

files.description: files lists all files that are part of this export format.

v1DBExportFormat.description: DBExportFormat describes a format (= a collection of files) for the database export.

name.description: name is the identifier for the file within the export format.

optional.description:
optional, when true, indicates that the file need not be present in a
restore body that uses this format.

files.description:
files lists every file that must be present and consumed in the restore
body, in the order they appear in the byte stream.

v1DBExportManifest.description:
A DB export manifest describes the file contents of a restore request. To prevent data loss, a manifest is always
interpreted as binding, i.e., the server must ensure that it will read and make use of every file listed in the
manifest, otherwise it must reject the request.

name.description:
The name of the file. This may or may not be a (relative) file path and up to the server to interpret.
For databases exported as ZIP files, this is the path relative to the root of the archive.

encoding.description: encoding specifies how the file data is encoded in the restore body.

encodedSize.description: encoded_size is the size of the encoded file data in bytes.

decodedSize.description: decoded_size is the expected size of the file after decoding.

decodedCrc32.description: The CRC32 (IEEE) checksum of the decoded(!) data.

v1DBExportManifestFile.description: A single file in the restore body.

id.description: An ID identifying the restore process. Auto-assigned.

header.description: The header with which the restore was initiated.

startTime.description: The time at which the restore process was started.

initiatingUserName.description: The user who initiated the database restore process.

v1DBRestoreProcessMetadata.description:
The metadata of an ongoing or completed restore process. This is the *static* metadata, which will not change
(i.e., it is not a status).

metadata.description: metadata contains the static metadata for this restore process.

attemptId.description:
attempt_id identifies the current restore attempt (a process may be
interrupted and resumed multiple times).

state.description: state is the current lifecycle state of the restore process.

resumeInfo.description:
resume_info is only populated when state is PAUSED and contains the
byte offset at which the process can be resumed.

only populated if state is PAUSED

error.description:
error is only populated when state is COMPLETED and the restore was
unsuccessful.

only populated when state is COMPLETED

bytesRead.description:
bytes_read is an approximate count of payload bytes consumed so far.

Payload bytes read so far (approximate)

filesProcessed.description:
files_processed is an approximate count of manifest files applied so far.

Files processed so far (approximate)

v1DBRestoreProcessStatusState.description:
State describes the lifecycle stage of a database restore process.

 - NOT_STARTED: NOT_STARTED indicates the restore has been registered but not yet begun.
 - IN_PROGRESS: IN_PROGRESS indicates the restore is actively reading and applying data.
 - PAUSED: PAUSED indicates the restore was interrupted and can be resumed.
 - COMPLETED: COMPLETED indicates the restore finished; check the error field to
determine success or failure.

successful if error is empty, unsuccessful otherwise

formatName.description: The name of the database export format. Mandatory.

manifest.description:
A manifest of all the files in the restore data. The remainder of a restore request body (without the header),
independent of the export format, is always a concatenation of all encoded file data, with no separators in
between (determined purely by the order and sizes listed in the manifest).

localFile.description: Information about the local file with which the restore was initiated. Optional.

activeStatus.description:
active_status is the status of the currently active restore process,
or empty if no restore is in progress.

formats.description: formats lists the export formats supported by this Central instance.

supportedEncodings.description:
supported_encodings lists the encoding types (e.g. deflate) accepted
for file data in restore request bodies.

resumeInfo.description:
resume_info contains the byte offset from which the interrupted restore
can be resumed.

SimpleAccessScopeRules.description:
Each element of any repeated field is an individual rule. Rules are
joined by logical OR: if there exists a rule allowing resource `x`,
`x` is in the access scope.

storageSetBasedLabelSelector.description:
SetBasedLabelSelector only allows set-based label requirements.

- NONE: Scan all images via central services except for images from the OCP integrated registry
 - ALL: Scan all images via the secured clusters
 - SPECIFIC: Scan images that match `registries` or are from the OCP integrated registry via the secured clusters
otherwise scan via central services

defaultClusterId.title:
The default cluster to delegate ad-hoc requests to if no match found in registries, not used
if `enabled for` is NONE

registries.description:
If `enabled for` is NONE registries has no effect.

If `ALL` registries directs ad-hoc requests to the specified secured clusters if the path matches.

If `SPECIFIC` registries directs ad-hoc requests to the specified secured clusters just like with `ALL`,
but in addition images that match the specified paths will be scanned locally by the secured clusters
(images from the OCP integrated registry are always scanned locally). Images that do not match a path
will be scanned via central services

source, etc.) as a peer in a baseline connection check.

v1NetworkBaselinePeerStatus.description:
NetworkBaselinePeerStatus pairs a peer connection with its baseline status
(BASELINE or ANOMALOUS) relative to the deployment's network baseline.

v1NetworkBaselinePeerStatusStatus.title:
Status of this peer connection. As of now we only have two statuses:
  - BASELINE: the connection is in the current deployment baseline
  - ANOMALOUS: the connection is not recognized by the current deployment baseline

entity.description:
The peer entity of the connection. This can be constructed from the
entity object of the networkgraph API. Only the ID and type are required.

port.description: The port and protocol of the destination of the given connection.

ingress.description:
A boolean representing whether the query is for an ingress or egress
connection. This is defined with respect to the current deployment.
Thus:
  - If the connection in question is in the outEdges of the current deployment,
    this should be false.
  - If it is in the outEdges of the peer deployment, this
    should be true.

v1NetworkBaselineStatusPeer.description:
NetworkBaselineStatusPeer describes a single peer connection to be checked
against a deployment's network baseline.

statuses.description: statuses contains the classification result for each input peer, in the same order.

entity.description: entity contains the CIDR block and optional name for the new external source.

NetworkGraphServiceCreateExternalNetwork.description:
CreateNetworkEntityRequest describes a new user-defined external network
entity (CIDR block) scoped to a specific cluster.

name.description: name is the new display name for the entity.

NetworkGraphServicePatchExternalNetworkE.description: PatchNetworkEntityRequest renames an existing user-defined external network entity.

clusterId.title: Need the clusterID as that is part of the key in RocksDB

updatedAt.title:
This field is set by Central everytime a flow is upserted.
This should not be set by Sensor.
For more context: https://github.com/stackrox/stackrox/pull/14483

srcEntity.title: The source deployment ID of the network flow

dstEntity.title: The destination deployment ID of the network flow

dstPort.description: may be 0 if not applicable (e.g., icmp).

entity.description: entity is the external network entity.

flowsCount.description: flowsCount is the total number of observed flows involving this entity.

v1ExternalNetworkFlowMetadata.description:
ExternalNetworkFlowMetadata summarizes observed flow counts for a single
external network entity.

entities.description:
entities is the paginated list of external entities with their flow counts,
sorted by entity ID for deterministic pagination.

totalEntities.description: total_entities is the total number of external entities before pagination.

v1GetExternalNetworkFlowsMetadataRespons.description:
GetExternalNetworkFlowsMetadataResponse contains per-entity flow counts for
all external entities with observed flows.

entity.description: entity is the external network entity these flows are associated with.

totalFlows.description: total_flows is the total number of matching flows before pagination.

flows.description: flows is the paginated list of observed network flows.

v1GetExternalNetworkFlowsResponse.description:
GetExternalNetworkFlowsResponse contains flows between cluster deployments
and a single external network entity.

port.description: port is the destination port of the connection.

protocol.description: protocol is the Layer-4 protocol (TCP, UDP, etc.) of the connection.

lastActiveTimestamp.description: last_active_timestamp is the most recent time this connection was observed.

v1NetworkEdgeProperties.description:
NetworkEdgeProperties describes a single observed connection on a specific
port and protocol.

v1NetworkEdgePropertiesBundle.description:
NetworkEdgePropertiesBundle groups all connection properties observed on a
single directed edge between two network nodes.

epoch.description:
epoch is a monotonically increasing counter that increments whenever the
underlying network policy graph changes. Clients can compare epochs to
detect staleness without re-fetching the full graph.

nodes.description:
nodes is the list of network entities in the graph. Edge indices in
out_edges refer to positions in this list.

v1NetworkGraph.description:
NetworkGraph is the response to GetNetworkGraph, containing a snapshot of
observed network flows within a cluster.

query.description: query filters the scope using StackRox search syntax (e.g. "Namespace:production").

v1NetworkGraphScope.description:
NetworkGraphScope restricts the network graph to a subset of deployments
matching the given search query.

entity.description: entity identifies the network entity (deployment, CIDR block, internet, etc.).

internetAccess.description: internet_access is true if this node has observed flows to/from the internet.

policyIds.description:
policy_ids lists the IDs of NetworkPolicies that select this deployment.
Populated only when include_policies is set in the request.

nonIsolatedIngress.description:
non_isolated_ingress is true when no ingress NetworkPolicy selects this deployment,
meaning all ingress traffic is allowed.

nonIsolatedEgress.description:
non_isolated_egress is true when no egress NetworkPolicy selects this deployment,
meaning all egress traffic is allowed.

queryMatch.description:
query_match is true if this node matched the filter query in the request
(as opposed to appearing only as a neighbor of a matching node).

outEdges.description:
out_edges maps the index of a destination node in the nodes list to the
set of observed connection properties on that edge.

v1NetworkNode.description:
NetworkNode represents a single entity (deployment, external source, or
internet) in the network graph.

config.description: config is the new network graph configuration. Required.

v1PutNetworkGraphConfigRequest.description: PutNetworkGraphConfigRequest wraps the new network graph configuration to persist.

GenerateNetworkPoliciesRequestDeleteExis.description:
- NONE: Do not delete any existing network policies.
 - GENERATED_ONLY: Delete any existing *auto-generated* network policies.
 - ALL: Delete all existing network policies in the respective namespace.

modification.description: modification describes the policies to create/update and the policies to delete.

NetworkPolicyServiceApplyNetworkPolicyYa.description:
ApplyNetworkPolicyYamlForDeploymentRequest applies a network policy
modification to the cluster of the specified deployment and records an
undo record keyed by deployment ID.

deleteExisting.description:
delete_existing controls whether any existing baseline-generated policy for
this deployment is included in the modification's delete list.

includePorts.description: include_ports controls whether per-port details are reflected in the generated rules.

NetworkPolicyServiceGetBaselineGenerated.description:
GetBaselineGeneratedPolicyForDeploymentRequest requests auto-generation of
a NetworkPolicy for a specific deployment based on its network baseline.

modification.description:
modification contains the YAML for newly generated policies and references
to policies that should be deleted, per the delete_existing mode.

v1GenerateNetworkPoliciesResponse.description:
Next available tag: 2
GenerateNetworkPoliciesResponse contains the generated NetworkPolicy YAML
modification ready to be applied or previewed.

allowedPeers.description:
allowed_peers is the list of permitted peer connections, including port,
protocol, and ingress/egress direction for each peer.

v1GetAllowedPeersFromCurrentPolicyForDep.description:
GetAllowedPeersFromCurrentPolicyForDeploymentResponse lists all network
peers that are currently permitted to communicate with a deployment based
on the active NetworkPolicies.

modification.description: modification contains the generated policy YAML and any policies to delete.

v1GetBaselineGeneratedPolicyForDeploymen.description:
GetBaselineGeneratedPolicyForDeploymentResponse contains the generated
NetworkPolicy modification for a specific deployment.

entity.description: entity is the peer network entity.

properties.description: properties are the connection properties (port, protocol, direction) for this peer.

v1GetDiffFlowsGroupedFlow.description:
GetDiffFlowsGroupedFlow groups connection properties for a single peer in
one of the two compared policy states (added or removed).

entity.description: entity is the peer network entity.

added.description: added lists connection properties that would be newly permitted in the new state.

removed.description: removed lists connection properties that would no longer be permitted.

unchanged.description: unchanged lists connection properties permitted in both states.

v1GetDiffFlowsReconciledFlow.description:
GetDiffFlowsReconciledFlow captures the connection-level diff for a single
peer between two network policy states.

added.description: added contains peers that are permitted in the new state but not in the current state.

removed.description: removed contains peers that are permitted in the current state but not in the new state.

reconciled.description: reconciled contains peers present in both states, with per-connection property diffs.

v1GetDiffFlowsResponse.description:
GetDiffFlowsResponse describes the diff between two sets of allowed peers
for a deployment (e.g. current policy vs. baseline-generated policy, or
current policy vs. undo state).

undoRecord.description: undo_record describes the original modification and the change needed to revert it.

v1GetUndoModificationForDeploymentRespon.description:
GetUndoModificationForDeploymentResponse contains the undo record for the
last network policy modification applied via ApplyNetworkPolicyYamlForDeployment.

undoRecord.description:
undo_record describes the original modification and the modification
needed to revert it.

v1GetUndoModificationResponse.description:
GetUndoModificationResponse contains the undo record for the last applied
network policy modification to a cluster.

nodeDiffs.description: node_diffs maps a node ID to the connectivity changes for that node.

v1NetworkGraphDiff.description:
NetworkGraphDiff summarizes which edges were added or removed between two
network graph states.

epoch.description:
epoch is a monotonically increasing counter. Clients can poll this to
detect when the graph needs to be refreshed.

v1NetworkGraphEpoch.description:
NetworkGraphEpoch is a version counter for the network policy graph of a
cluster. It increments whenever the underlying network policies change.

policyIds.description: policy_ids lists NetworkPolicy IDs that select this deployment in the new state.

outEdges.description: out_edges maps a destination node ID to the edge properties in the new state.

nonIsolatedIngress.description: non_isolated_ingress is true when no ingress policy selects this node in the new state.

nonIsolatedEgress.description: non_isolated_egress is true when no egress policy selects this node in the new state.

v1NetworkNodeDiff.description:
NetworkNodeDiff captures the change to a single network node's connectivity
between two graph states (e.g. current vs. simulated).

policy.description: policy is the network policy in its simulated state (nil if DELETED).

status.description: status indicates how this policy is affected by the proposed modification.

oldPolicy.description:
old_policy is the current (pre-simulation) version of the policy.
Populated when status is MODIFIED or DELETED.

if status is MODIFIED or DELETED, this contains the previous network policy.

v1NetworkPolicyInSimulation.description:
NetworkPolicyInSimulation represents a single network policy in the context
of a simulation, annotated with whether it is unchanged, added, modified,
or deleted by the proposed modification.

v1NetworkPolicyInSimulationStatus.description:
- UNCHANGED: UNCHANGED means this policy is not affected by the proposed modification.
 - MODIFIED: MODIFIED means this policy is replaced by a new version in the simulation.
 - ADDED: ADDED means this policy does not currently exist and would be created.
 - DELETED: DELETED means this policy would be removed by the modification.

simulatedGraph.description:
simulated_graph is the network connectivity graph after applying the
proposed modification.

policies.description:
policies is the full list of network policies in the simulation with their
change status (UNCHANGED, ADDED, MODIFIED, DELETED).

added.description:
added contains nodes and edges that would be added by the modification.
Only populated when include_node_diff is true and the modification has changes.

removed.description:
removed contains nodes and edges that would be removed by the modification.
Only populated when include_node_diff is true and the modification has changes.

v1SimulateNetworkGraphResponse.description:
SimulateNetworkGraphResponse contains the results of a network policy
simulation, including the simulated graph, the policy states, and optional
edge diffs.

disable controls the direction: if true, the specified notifiers are removed
from the policy; if false, they are added.

disabled.description:
disabled sets whether the policy is enabled (false) or disabled (true).
When disabled, the policy does not fire alerts or enforce actions.

PolicyServicePatchPolicyBody.description:
PatchPolicyRequest is used to make partial updates to a policy, currently
limited to toggling the enabled/disabled state.

name.description: Name of the policy.  Must be unique.

description.description: Free-form text description of this policy.

remediation.description: Describes how to remediate a violation of this policy.

disabled.description: Toggles whether or not this policy will be executing and actively firing alerts.

categories.description: List of categories that this policy falls under.  Category names must already exist in Central.

lifecycleStages.description: Describes which policy lifecylce stages this policy applies to.  Choices are DEPLOY, BUILD, and RUNTIME.

eventSource.title: Describes which events should trigger execution of this policy

exclusions.description: Define deployments or images that should be excluded from this policy.

scope.description: Defines clusters, namespaces, and deployments that should be included in this policy.  No scopes defined includes everything.

severity.description: Defines how severe a violation from this policy is.  Possible values are UNSET_SEVERITY, LOW_SEVERITY, MEDIUM_SEVERITY, HIGH_SEVERITY, and CRITICAL_SEVERITY.

enforcementActions.description:
FAIL_DEPLOYMENT_CREATE_ENFORCEMENT takes effect only if admission control webhook is configured to enforce on object creates/updates.
FAIL_KUBE_REQUEST_ENFORCEMENT takes effect only if admission control webhook is enabled to listen on exec and port-forward events.
FAIL_DEPLOYMENT_UPDATE_ENFORCEMENT takes effect only if admission control webhook is configured to enforce on object updates.
Lists the enforcement actions to take when a violation from this policy is identified.  Possible value are UNSET_ENFORCEMENT, SCALE_TO_ZERO_ENFORCEMENT, UNSATISFIABLE_NODE_CONSTRAINT_ENFORCEMENT, KILL_POD_ENFORCEMENT, FAIL_BUILD_ENFORCEMENT, FAIL_KUBE_REQUEST_ENFORCEMENT, FAIL_DEPLOYMENT_CREATE_ENFORCEMENT, and. FAIL_DEPLOYMENT_UPDATE_ENFORCEMENT.

notifiers.description: List of IDs of the notifiers that should be triggered when a violation from this policy is identified.  IDs should be in the form of a UUID and are found through the Central API.

policySections.description: PolicySections define the violation criteria for this policy.

criteriaLocked.description: Read-only field. If true, the policy's criteria fields are rendered read-only.

mitreVectorsLocked.description: Read-only field. If true, the policy's MITRE ATT&CK fields are rendered read-only.

isDefault.description: Read-only field. Indicates the policy is a default policy if true and a custom policy if false.

We do backwards-compatibility checks on objects in the storge folder and those checks should be applied to this object

pending.description: pending is true if the job is still running and result is not yet available.

result.description: result is populated once pending is false and contains the dry-run output.

alerts.description:

the specific violation messages for each.

deployments without persisting or enforcing the policy.

policyIds.description:
policy_ids is the list of UUIDs of the policies to export.
All IDs must exist; if any are missing the request returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.

v1ExportPoliciesRequest.description: ExportPoliciesRequest selects a set of policies to export by ID.

excludePolicy.description: If set to true, policy is excluded from the response.

policy.description: policy is the full policy object. Omitted if options.exclude_policy is true.

vectors.description:
vectors is the list of MITRE ATT&CK tactic/technique pairs associated with
the policy, expanded from the policy's mitre_attack_vectors field using the
MITRE ATT&CK knowledge base.

v1GetPolicyMitreVectorsResponse.description: GetPolicyMitreVectorsResponse returns a policy and its expanded MITRE ATT&CK vectors.

overwrite.description:
overwrite controls conflict resolution: if true, an imported policy whose
name or ID already exists in Central will replace the existing policy.
If false, a conflict causes that policy's import to fail.

v1ImportPoliciesMetadata.description: ImportPoliciesMetadata controls how conflicts are resolved during import.

metadata.description: metadata controls import behavior such as overwrite semantics.

policies.description: policies is the list of policy definitions to import.

v1ImportPoliciesRequest.description: ImportPoliciesRequest submits one or more policies for import into Central.

responses.description: responses contains one entry per submitted policy, in the same order.

allSucceeded.description:
all_succeeded is true only when every policy in the batch was imported
successfully. Use this as a quick summary check.

v1ImportPoliciesResponse.description: ImportPoliciesResponse reports the overall outcome of an import batch.

message.description: message is a human-readable summary of the error.

type.description: type is a machine-readable error category (e.g. "duplicate_name", "validation_error").

duplicateName.description:
duplicate_name is set when the import failed because a policy with
the same name already exists and overwrite was not requested.

validationError.description: validation_error is set when the policy failed schema or semantic validation.

v1ImportPolicyError.description: ImportPolicyError describes why a single policy failed to import.

succeeded.description: succeeded is true when the policy was imported without errors.

policy.description:
policy is the policy as it was processed (post-validation, with internal
fields stripped). Returned whether or not the import succeeded.

errors.description:
errors lists all errors encountered for this policy. Non-empty only when
succeeded is false.

v1ImportPolicyResponse.description: ImportPolicyResponse reports the outcome for a single policy in an import batch.

policies.description: policies is the list of matching policies in ListPolicy (summary) form.

v1ListPoliciesResponse.description: ListPoliciesResponse contains a lightweight summary of each matching policy.

categories.description: categories is the sorted list of all category names assigned to at least one policy.

v1PolicyCategoriesResponse.description: PolicyCategoriesResponse holds the distinct set of policy categories in use.

search_params is a StackRox search query string
(e.g. "Image Registry:docker.io+CVE:CVE-2021-44228").
Search terms that cannot be represented as policy criteria are reported
in the response's altered_search_terms field.

policy is the draft policy generated from the search parameters.
It is not persisted; use PostPolicy to save it.

altered_search_terms lists search field names that were dropped or
partially converted because they have no direct policy criteria equivalent.

hasNestedFields.description:
has_nested_fields indicates whether any generated policy criteria use
nested field evaluation, which may affect how violations are detected.

v1PolicyFromSearchResponse.description:

along with metadata about search terms that could not be converted.

EffectiveAccessScope describes which clusters and namespaces are "in scope"

known clusters and namespaces, the result is EffectiveAccessScope.

excluded. If a node is included, all its child nodes are included.

v1SearchCategory.description:
SearchCategory identifies a resource type that can be queried through SearchService.

Not all categories are exposed via the global Search RPC — some are internal
or used only for autocomplete/options. The primary searchable categories are
ALERTS, DEPLOYMENTS, IMAGES, POLICIES, SECRETS, NAMESPACES, NODES, CLUSTERS,
SERVICE_ACCOUNTS, ROLES, ROLEBINDINGS, SUBJECTS, IMAGE_INTEGRATIONS, and POLICY_CATEGORIES.

Next available tag: 85

 - SEARCH_UNSET: SEARCH_UNSET means no category was specified. When passed to Search or
Autocomplete it causes the service to search across all supported categories.
 - ALERTS: ALERTS searches active and resolved policy-violation alerts.
 - IMAGES: IMAGES searches container images observed in the environment.
 - IMAGE_COMPONENTS: IMAGE_COMPONENTS searches individual software components (packages) within images.
 - POLICIES: POLICIES searches security policies defined in StackRox.
 - DEPLOYMENTS: DEPLOYMENTS searches Kubernetes Deployments, DaemonSets, StatefulSets, and other workloads.
 - PODS: PODS searches individual Kubernetes Pods.
 - SECRETS: SECRETS searches Kubernetes Secrets observed in secured clusters.
 - PROCESS_INDICATORS: PROCESS_INDICATORS searches runtime process execution events.
 - COMPLIANCE: COMPLIANCE searches compliance aggregation results across standards and controls.
 - CLUSTERS: CLUSTERS searches secured Kubernetes clusters registered with Central.
 - NAMESPACES: NAMESPACES searches Kubernetes namespaces across all secured clusters.
 - NODES: NODES searches Kubernetes nodes across all secured clusters.
 - NODE_COMPONENTS: NODE_COMPONENTS searches software components (packages) installed on nodes.
 - SERVICE_ACCOUNTS: SERVICE_ACCOUNTS searches Kubernetes ServiceAccount objects.
 - ROLES: ROLES searches Kubernetes Role and ClusterRole objects.
 - ROLEBINDINGS: ROLEBINDINGS searches Kubernetes RoleBinding and ClusterRoleBinding objects.
 - SUBJECTS: SUBJECTS searches users and groups that appear as subjects in RBAC bindings.
 - RISKS: RISKS searches risk scores associated with deployments.
 - VULNERABILITIES: VULNERABILITIES searches CVEs across images, nodes, and cluster components.
 - IMAGE_VULNERABILITIES: IMAGE_VULNERABILITIES searches CVEs found in container image components.
 - NODE_VULNERABILITIES: NODE_VULNERABILITIES searches CVEs found in node components.
 - POLICY_CATEGORIES: POLICY_CATEGORIES searches user-defined policy category labels.
 - IMAGE_INTEGRATIONS: IMAGE_INTEGRATIONS searches configured image registry integrations.
 - COLLECTIONS: COLLECTIONS searches resource collections (scoped sets of deployments).
 - IMAGE_COMPONENTS_V2: IMAGE_COMPONENTS_V2 is the v2 image components model used when the FlattenImageData feature flag is enabled.
 - IMAGE_VULNERABILITIES_V2: IMAGE_VULNERABILITIES_V2 is the v2 image vulnerabilities model used when the FlattenImageData feature flag is enabled.
 - IMAGES_V2: IMAGES_V2 is the v2 image model used when the FlattenImageData feature flag is enabled.

certificateOidcIssuer.description:
Certificate OIDC issuer to verify against.
This supports regular expressions following the RE2 syntax: https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax.
In case the certificate does not specify an OIDC issuer, you may use '.*' as the OIDC issuer. However,
it is recommended to use Fulcio compatible certificates according to the specification:
https://github.com/sigstore/fulcio/blob/main/docs/certificate-specification.md.

certificateIdentity.description:
Certificate identity to verify against.
This supports regular expressions following the RE2 syntax: https://github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax.
In case the certificate does not specify an identity, you may use '.*' as the identity. However, it is
recommended to use Fulcio compatible certificates according to the specification:
https://github.com/sigstore/fulcio/blob/main/docs/certificate-specification.md.

storageCosignCertificateVerification.description:
Holds all verification data for verifying certificates attached to cosign signatures.
If only the certificate is given, the Fulcio trusted root chain will be assumed and verified against.
If only the chain is given, this will be used over the Fulcio trusted root chain for verification.
If no certificate or chain is given, the Fulcio trusted root chain will be assumed and verified against.
